papers.title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence for Financial Integral Equations: A Fixed-Point Neural Operator Approach
papers.summary: The explainable artificial intelligence is used to analyze the stochastic Fredholm integral equations (SFIEs) and stochastic deep neural networks (SDNNs). The neural operator-based stochastic fixed point framework is used to develop SDNNs. The solution of an SFIE is obtained through successive applications of an integral operator, and this iterative structure naturally resembles the layered architecture of a neural network. The associated nonlinear versions of SFIE and SDNN are discussed. The SFIE and SDNN are used to solve the Black-Scholes equation, contagion dynamics of financial networks, and the Merten jump diffusion equation. It is observed that the results obtained through SFIE and SDNN for all the applications agree well.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于神经算子的随机不动点框架，将可解释人工智能用于求解随机Fredholm积分方程，并应用于金融方程。
ai.motivation: 金融积分方程（如Black-Scholes）求解需要可解释且高效的方法，而传统数值方法缺乏透明性。
ai.method: 利用神经算子构建随机不动点框架，将积分算子的迭代过程类比为深度神经网络的层次结构，从而训练随机深度神经网络。
ai.result: 在Black-Scholes方程、金融网络传染动力学和Merton跳扩散方程上，SFIE与SDNN的结果吻合良好。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可解释的AI方法求解金融积分方程，帮助理解模型内部机制。

idea_spark.idea: 将积分算子迭代与神经网络层对应，利用神经算子学习不动点映射。
idea_spark.risk: 非线性版本的理论收敛性需进一步验证，且对高维问题可能面临计算瓶颈。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从神经网络层次结构与积分算子迭代的相似性出发，设计可解释的AI求解器。

en.tldr: Proposes a neural operator-based stochastic fixed-point framework that uses explainable AI to solve stochastic Fredholm integral equations, applied to financial equations.
en.motivation: Solving financial integral equations (e.g., Black-Scholes) requires interpretable and efficient methods, while traditional numerical approaches lack transparency.
en.method: Builds a stochastic fixed-point framework with neural operators, treating the iterative application of an integral operator as analogous to the layered architecture of a neural network to train stochastic deep neural networks.
en.result: The SFIE and SDNN results agree well for the Black-Scholes equation, financial network contagion dynamics, and the Merton jump diffusion equation.
en.help_to_user: Provides an explainable AI method for solving financial integral equations, aiding understanding of the model's internal mechanisms.

idea_spark.idea: Map integral operator iterations to neural network layers and learn the fixed-point mapping via neural operators.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical convergence for nonlinear versions needs further validation, and high-dimensional problems may face computational bottlenecks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws inspiration from the similarity between neural network layered architectures and iterative integral operators to design an explainable AI solver.

papers.title: BAss: Symbolic Reasoning in Abstract Dialectical Frameworks
papers.summary: We present BAss (BDD-based ADF symbolic solver), a novel analysis tool for Abstract Dialectical Frameworks (ADFs) based on Binary Decision Diagrams (BDDs). It supports the fully symbolic computation of all admissible, complete, and preferred interpretations, as well as two-valued and stable models of an ADFs. Our approach is inspired by the recently discovered equivalence between Boolean Networks (BNs) and ADFs by Heyninck et al. (2024) and Azpeitia et al. (2024), significantly extending current BDD-based tools bioLQM, AEON, and adf-bdd. We conducted experiments on a large-scale collection of real-world models from both the BN and ADF communities. Our results show that BAss dramatically outperforms previous BDD-based tools and is competitive (even significantly better in some cases) with state-of-the-art SAT/ASP-based methods, particularly in scenarios involving large solution spaces. Notably, BAss is able to enumerate all fixed points or minimal trap spaces of certain biological networks beyond the reach of existing tools, thereby enabling new analysis and case studies in systems biology. These results highlight the practical relevance of symbolic reasoning for complex real-world applications, particularly in systems biology and formal argumentation.

ai.tldr: 提出基于二元决策图的抽象辩证框架符号求解器BAss，支持完全符号计算所有可接受、完全和优先解释以及两值和稳定模型，在生物网络和形式论证领域显著优于现有工具。
ai.motivation: 现有ADF求解工具效率有限，尤其在大解空间场景下；受布尔网络与ADF等价性启发，希望利用BDD实现高效符号推理。
ai.method: 基于二元决策图（BDD）实现ADF的完全符号计算，扩展了bioLQM、AEON和adf-bdd等工具，利用BDD高效表示和操作布尔函数。
ai.result: 在真实世界大规模模型上，BAss大幅超越先前BDD工具，与SAT/ASP方法竞争力强，尤其在枚举生物网络不动点和最小陷阱空间方面突破现有工具限制。
ai.help_to_user: 为系统生物学和形式论证研究提供高效符号推理工具，支持大规模模型分析。

idea_spark.idea: 将ADF与布尔网络等价性用于跨领域模型转换，实现统一符号求解。
idea_spark.risk: BDD规模可能随变量数指数增长，需优化变量排序或采用近似方法。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用形式化方法（如BDD）解决生物网络分析中的组合爆炸问题，启发其他复杂系统符号推理研究。

en.tldr: We present BAss, a BDD-based symbolic solver for Abstract Dialectical Frameworks, supporting fully symbolic computation of all admissible, complete, and preferred interpretations as well as two-valued and stable models, significantly outperforming previous tools on real-world biological and argumentation models.
en.motivation: Existing ADF solvers are inefficient for large solution spaces; inspired by the equivalence between Boolean Networks and ADFs, we aim to leverage BDDs for efficient symbolic reasoning.
en.method: We implement a fully symbolic computation of ADF semantics using Binary Decision Diagrams, extending existing BDD-based tools like bioLQM, AEON, and adf-bdd.
en.result: On large-scale real-world models, BAss dramatically outperforms previous BDD tools and is competitive with SAT/ASP methods, especially in enumerating fixed points and minimal trap spaces of biological networks beyond the reach of existing tools.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient symbolic reasoning tool for systems biology and formal argumentation, enabling analysis of large models.

idea_spark.idea: Exploit the equivalence between ADFs and Boolean Networks for cross-domain model conversion and unified symbolic solving.
idea_spark.risk: BDD size may grow exponentially with the number of variables; variable ordering or approximation methods may be needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using formal methods like BDDs to tackle combinatorial explosion in biological network analysis inspires symbolic reasoning for other complex systems.

papers.title: DeepPropNet: an operator learning-based predictor for thermal plasma properties
papers.summary: Thermal plasma properties play a critical role in plasma simulations and plasma-related applications. However, their strong nonlinear dependence on temperature, pressure, and gas composition makes accurate and efficient evaluation challenging. In this work, an operator learning-based model, termed DeepPropNet, is proposed for fast prediction of thermodynamic and transport properties of thermal plasmas. Two architectures are developed, including a single-property model (S-DeepPropNet) and a Mixture of Experts (MoE)-based multi-property model (MoE-DeepPropNet). The proposed models learn the nonlinear mapping from plasma operating conditions to physical properties based on high-fidelity datasets. The MoE architecture enables efficient multi-property prediction within a unified framework. Predictions are performed for binary SF6-N2 and ternary C4F7N-CO2-O2 mixtures. The results show that the proposed models achieve high accuracy, with relative L2 errors on the order of 10-3 to 10-2, while maintaining strong generalization capability under unseen conditions. The applicability of DeepPropNet is further demonstrated by coupling with finite volume method (FVM) and physics-informed neural networks (PINNs). The results indicate that DeepPropNet provides an efficient and scalable approach for plasma property prediction and plasma simulations.

ai.tldr: 提出基于算子学习的DeepPropNet模型，用于快速预测热等离子体的热力学和输运性质。
ai.motivation: 热等离子体性质对温度、压力和气体成分高度非线性依赖，传统方法准确评估困难且效率低。
ai.method: 开发了单性质模型S-DeepPropNet和基于混合专家（MoE）的多性质模型MoE-DeepPropNet，学习从工况到物理性质的非线性映射。
ai.result: 在SF6-N2和C4F7N-CO2-O2混合物上，相对L2误差达10⁻³~10⁻²，泛化能力强，并与FVM和PINN耦合验证了适用性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速获取热等离子体性质，加速等离子体仿真和相关应用。

idea_spark.idea: 将MoE架构用于多性质联合预测，减少模型数量并保持精度。
idea_spark.risk: 训练数据覆盖范围不足时，外推预测可能不准确。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合物理信息（如PINN）可进一步提升模型可靠性和可解释性。

en.tldr: Proposes DeepPropNet, an operator learning-based model for fast prediction of thermodynamic and transport properties of thermal plasmas.
en.motivation: Thermal plasma properties exhibit strong nonlinear dependence on temperature, pressure, and gas composition, making accurate and efficient evaluation challenging.
en.method: Develops a single-property model (S-DeepPropNet) and a Mixture of Experts (MoE)-based multi-property model (MoE-DeepPropNet) to learn the nonlinear mapping from operating conditions to physical properties.
en.result: Achieves relative L2 errors on the order of 10⁻³ to 10⁻² on SF6-N2 and C4F7N-CO2-O2 mixtures, with strong generalization, and demonstrates applicability by coupling with FVM and PINNs.
en.help_to_user: Enables fast and accurate prediction of thermal plasma properties, accelerating plasma simulations and related applications.

idea_spark.idea: Using MoE architecture for joint multi-property prediction reduces model count while maintaining accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: Extrapolation may be inaccurate if training data coverage is insufficient.
idea_spark.inspiration: Incorporating physical information (e.g., PINNs) can further improve model reliability and interpretability.

papers.title: A stochastic agent-based extension of the GSM2 model for particle therapy: cell-cycle dynamics, dose-rate dependence, and fractionation effects
papers.summary: Accurately linking microscopic energy deposition from ionizing radiation to emergent biological outcomes remains a central challenge in radiobiological modelling, particularly when stochastic damage induction, cell-cycle dynamics, and spatial organisation within irradiated tissues must be treated explicitly and consistently across scales. To address this, we introduce a stochastic agent-based radiobiological modelling framework for simulating biological response to particle irradiation, developed as an explicit single-cell extension of the Generalized Stochastic Microdosimetric Model (GSM2). Each cell is represented as an autonomous agent whose internal state, including DNA lesion counts, cell-cycle phase, and oxygenation level, evolves according to a continuous-time Markov chain driven by GSM2 transition rates. Radiation-induced damage induction, repair, misrepair, cell-cycle progression, proliferation, and migration are treated as competing stochastic events resolved through a next-event, event-driven algorithm, which provides computationally efficient scaling with system size while preserving full single-cell resolution.   The framework is applied to three-dimensional tumour spheroids irradiated with 1H and 12C ions across a range of energies and dose rates. We characterise the spatiotemporal evolution of cell-cycle phase composition and spheroid volume following irradiation, and examine the dependence of cell survival on dose rate over four orders of magnitude. Several empirically established trends in biological response, including the dose-rate dependence of cell survival, its attenuation at high LET, and the inverse dose rate effect in split-dose irradiation, emerge from the model through the explicit coupling of particle arrivals, damage accumulation, and repair kinetics, without recourse to empirical correction factors as typically done.

ai.tldr: 提出一个基于随机智能体的放射生物学模型，扩展GSM2，模拟粒子治疗中细胞周期、剂量率及分次效应。
ai.motivation: 准确连接微观能量沉积与宏观生物效应，特别是处理随机损伤、细胞周期和空间组织。
ai.method: 每个细胞作为自主智能体，内部状态（DNA损伤、细胞周期、氧合）由连续时间马尔可夫链驱动，采用事件驱动算法。
ai.result: 模型再现了细胞存活对剂量率的依赖、高LET下的衰减以及分次照射中的逆剂量率效应。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解粒子治疗中细胞响应的时空动态，优化治疗计划。

idea_spark.idea: 结合实验数据校准模型参数，预测个体化放疗反应。
idea_spark.risk: 模型复杂度高，参数校准需大量实验数据。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将细胞视为智能体，模拟群体行为，启发多尺度生物系统建模。

en.tldr: A stochastic agent-based extension of GSM2 for particle therapy modeling cell-cycle dynamics, dose-rate dependence, and fractionation effects.
en.motivation: To accurately link microscopic energy deposition to emergent biological outcomes, explicitly treating stochastic damage, cell-cycle dynamics, and spatial organization.
en.method: Each cell is an autonomous agent with internal state (DNA lesions, cell-cycle phase, oxygenation) evolving via continuous-time Markov chain driven by GSM2 rates, using an event-driven algorithm.
en.result: The model reproduces dose-rate dependence of cell survival, attenuation at high LET, and inverse dose-rate effect in split-dose irradiation.
en.help_to_user: Provides insights into spatiotemporal cell response in particle therapy, aiding treatment optimization.

idea_spark.idea: Calibrate model parameters with experimental data to predict individualized radiotherapy outcomes.
idea_spark.risk: High model complexity requires extensive data for parameter estimation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Treating cells as agents to simulate collective behavior inspires multi-scale biological modeling.

papers.title: Lipschitz Stability of boundary measurements for unknown constant polygonal conductivity inclusions
papers.summary: In this work, we derive the Lipschitz stability for conductivities of unknown constants as well as unknown polygonal inclusions from the Dirichlet-to-Neumann map.

ai.tldr: 本文从狄利克雷到诺伊曼映射出发，推导了未知常数以及未知多边形电导率夹杂物的Lipschitz稳定性。
ai.motivation: 研究边界测量对未知多边形电导率夹杂物的Lipschitz稳定性，以提升反问题求解的鲁棒性。
ai.method: 基于狄利克雷到诺伊曼映射，分析电导率参数与夹杂物几何的稳定性。
ai.result: 获得了未知常数电导率及多边形夹杂物的Lipschitz稳定性估计。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解边界测量对电导率反问题的稳定性条件。

idea_spark.idea: 利用多边形夹杂物的几何先验信息，设计更稳定的反演算法。
idea_spark.risk: 多边形假设可能限制实际复杂形状的应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合几何正则化与稳定性分析，提升反问题求解的可靠性。

en.tldr: This work derives Lipschitz stability for conductivities of unknown constants and unknown polygonal inclusions from the Dirichlet-to-Neumann map.
en.motivation: To study the Lipschitz stability of boundary measurements for unknown polygonal conductivity inclusions, enhancing robustness in inverse problem solving.
en.method: Analysis based on the Dirichlet-to-Neumann map to derive stability estimates for conductivity parameters and inclusion geometry.
en.result: Lipschitz stability estimates are obtained for unknown constant conductivities and polygonal inclusions.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand stability conditions for inverse conductivity problems from boundary measurements.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage geometric priors of polygonal inclusions to design more stable inversion algorithms.
idea_spark.risk: Polygonal assumption may limit applicability to complex real-world shapes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine geometric regularization with stability analysis to improve reliability of inverse problem solutions.

papers.title: Nonlinear evolution equations with a non-Lipschitz perturbation: convergence of successive approximations and uniqueness of solutions
papers.summary: This paper investigates the existence and uniqueness of solutions for a nonlinear evolution equation governed by an m-accretive operator A in a Banach space, presenting a perturbation term that does not satisfy the Lipschitz condition.

ai.tldr: 本文研究Banach空间中由m-增生算子控制的非线性发展方程，在扰动项不满足Lipschitz条件时的解的存在唯一性。
ai.motivation: 经典Lipschitz扰动理论无法处理更一般的非线性扰动，需要推广存在唯一性结果。
ai.method: 采用逐次逼近法（Picard迭代）结合m-增生算子的性质进行证明。
ai.result: 证明了在非Lipschitz扰动下解的存在唯一性。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理非光滑或强非线性扰动的发展方程提供了理论保证。

idea_spark.idea: 将非Lipschitz扰动分解为有界部分与单调部分分别处理。
idea_spark.risk: 非Lipschitz项可能导致迭代不收敛或解不唯一。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴m-增生算子半群理论处理非光滑动力系统。

en.tldr: This paper investigates the existence and uniqueness of solutions for a nonlinear evolution equation governed by an m-accretive operator in a Banach space, with a perturbation term that does not satisfy the Lipschitz condition.
en.motivation: Classical Lipschitz perturbation theory cannot handle more general nonlinear perturbations, so existence and uniqueness results need to be extended.
en.method: The method of successive approximations (Picard iteration) combined with properties of m-accretive operators is employed.
en.result: Existence and uniqueness of solutions under non-Lipschitz perturbations are proved.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical guarantees for evolution equations with non-smooth or strongly nonlinear perturbations.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose the non-Lipschitz perturbation into a bounded part and a monotone part for separate treatment.
idea_spark.risk: Non-Lipschitz terms may cause iteration divergence or non-uniqueness of solutions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw on semigroup theory of m-accretive operators to handle non-smooth dynamical systems.

papers.title: Hyper-Dimensional Fingerprints as Molecular Representations
papers.summary: Computational molecular representations underpin virtual screening, property prediction, and materials discovery. Conventional fingerprints are efficient and deterministic but lose structural information through hash-based compression, particularly at low dimensionalities. Learned representations from graph neural networks recover this expressiveness but require task-specific training and substantial computational resources. Here we introduce hyperdimensional fingerprints (HDF), which replace the learned transformations of message-passing neural networks with algebraic operations on high-dimensional vectors, producing deterministic molecular representations without any training. Across diverse property prediction benchmarks, HDF outperforms conventional fingerprints in the majority of tasks while exhibiting greater consistency across datasets and models. Crucially, HDF embeddings preserve molecular similarity faithfully: at 32 dimensions, distances in HDF space achieve a 0.9 Pearson correlation with graph edit distance, compared to 0.55 for Morgan fingerprints at equivalent size. This structural fidelity persists at low dimensions where hash-based methods degrade, allowing simple nearest-neighbor regression to remain predictive with as few as 64 components. We further demonstrate the practical impact in Bayesian molecular optimization, where HDF-based surrogate models achieve substantially improved sample efficiency in regimes where Morgan fingerprints perform comparably to random search. HDF thus provides a general-purpose, training-free alternative to conventional molecular fingerprints, suggesting that the information loss long accepted as inherent to fixed-length fingerprints is a limitation of the hash-based encoding scheme rather than the fingerprint paradigm itself.

ai.tldr: 提出超维指纹（HDF），一种无需训练、确定性的分子表示方法，在低维度下保持结构保真度，优于传统指纹。
ai.motivation: 传统指纹通过哈希压缩丢失结构信息，尤其在低维度下；而图神经网络表示虽表达力强但需任务特定训练和大量计算。
ai.method: 用高维向量上的代数运算替代消息传递神经网络的学得变换，生成确定性分子表示，无需训练。
ai.result: 在多数属性预测任务中优于传统指纹，32维时与图编辑距离的皮尔逊相关系数达0.9（摩根指纹仅0.55），64维下最近邻回归仍有效；在贝叶斯分子优化中显著提升样本效率。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种通用、无需训练的分子指纹替代方案，适用于虚拟筛选、属性预测和材料发现。

idea_spark.idea: 将超维计算与分子指纹结合，利用高维空间的代数性质实现无需学习的结构保持。
idea_spark.risk: 高维向量计算可能带来存储和计算开销，且对超参数（如维度）敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受超维计算（HDC）和传统指纹的启发，用代数操作替代学习，保留确定性同时提升保真度。

en.tldr: Proposes hyperdimensional fingerprints (HDF), a training-free deterministic molecular representation that preserves structural fidelity at low dimensions, outperforming conventional fingerprints.
en.motivation: Conventional fingerprints lose structural information via hash-based compression, especially at low dimensions; learned GNN representations are expressive but require task-specific training and high computation.
en.method: Replaces learned transformations of message-passing neural networks with algebraic operations on high-dimensional vectors, producing deterministic representations without training.
en.result: Outperforms conventional fingerprints on most property prediction tasks; achieves 0.9 Pearson correlation with graph edit distance at 32 dimensions (vs. 0.55 for Morgan); nearest-neighbor regression remains predictive with 64 components; improves sample efficiency in Bayesian molecular optimization.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general-purpose, training-free alternative to conventional molecular fingerprints for virtual screening, property prediction, and materials discovery.

idea_spark.idea: Combines hyperdimensional computing with molecular fingerprints, leveraging algebraic properties of high-dimensional spaces for learning-free structure preservation.
idea_spark.risk: High-dimensional vectors may incur storage and computational overhead, and sensitivity to hyperparameters like dimensionality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by hyperdimensional computing (HDC) and traditional fingerprints, replacing learned transformations with algebraic operations to retain determinism while improving fidelity.

papers.title: Renormalized Solutions for a Class of Nonlinear Parabolic Equation with a Lower Order Term and Variable Exponents
papers.summary:
We consider a class of nonlinear parabolic equations   \[   \dfrac{\partial}{\partial t} b(u)-
abla \cdot (A(x,t,u,
abla u))+H(x,t,
abla u)=f ,   \]   where $H$ is a nonlinear lower order term satisfied the Carath$\acute{e}$odory condition and   \[   \left\lvert H(x,t,
abla u)ightvert\leqslant g(x,t)\left\lvert 
abla uightvert^{δ(x)}   \]   with   \[   δ(x)= rac{p(x)(N+1)-N}{(N+2)(p(x)-1)}(p^--1) \quad 	ext{and} \quad p^-=\underset{x\in arΩ}{min}\,p(x).   \]   By virtue of truncation metheod,the monotone operator theory   and a gradient estimate we prove existence of renormalized solutions without coercivity condition on lower order term in the framework of variable exponents.

ai.tldr: 研究了一类带有低阶项和变指数的非线性抛物方程，通过截断法、单调算子理论和梯度估计，在低阶项无强制性的条件下证明了重整化解的存在性。
ai.motivation: 现有理论对低阶项通常要求强制性条件，本文旨在去除这一限制，拓展变指数框架下重整化解的存在性结果。
ai.method: 采用截断法处理非线性项，结合单调算子理论和梯度估计技术。
ai.result: 在低阶项满足Carathéodory条件及特定增长约束时，证明了重整化解的存在性。
ai.help_to_user: 为变指数非线性抛物方程的理论分析提供了新的存在性工具，尤其适用于低阶项无强制性的情形。

idea_spark.idea: 尝试将重整化解概念应用于带有变指数的时间分数阶扩散方程。
idea_spark.risk: 变指数框架下的紧性论证可能因指数变化而失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受低阶项无强制性条件的启发，可探索更弱的增长条件对解的正则性影响。

en.tldr: We prove existence of renormalized solutions for a class of nonlinear parabolic equations with a lower order term and variable exponents, without coercivity on the lower order term, using truncation, monotone operator theory, and gradient estimates.
en.motivation: Existing theory often requires coercivity on the lower order term; this work removes that restriction to extend existence results in variable exponent spaces.
en.method: Truncation method combined with monotone operator theory and gradient estimates.
en.result: Existence of renormalized solutions is established under Carathéodory condition and specific growth constraints on the lower order term.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new existence tool for nonlinear parabolic equations with variable exponents, especially when the lower order term lacks coercivity.

idea_spark.idea: Apply the renormalized solution concept to time-fractional diffusion equations with variable exponents.
idea_spark.risk: Compactness arguments may fail due to the variable exponent framework.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by removing coercivity, one could explore even weaker growth conditions and their effect on solution regularity.

papers.title: Existence and Uniqueness of Normalized Multi-peak Solutions for Coupled Nonlinear Schrödinger Systems
papers.summary: We consider the following two-component coupled nonlinear Schrödinger (CNLS) system: \[ \begin{cases} -Δu +(P(x) + λ) u=μ_1 u^3+βu v^2, & \text{in } \mathbb{R}^N,\\ -Δv +(Q(x) + λ) v =μ_2 v^3+βvu^2, & \text{in } \mathbb{R}^N \end{cases} \] with the mass constraint $\int_{\mathbb{R}^N} (u^2+v^2)\,dx = ρ^2$ for $N=2,3$, where $ρ>0$ is a parameter. By employing the Lyapunov-Schmidt reduction and local Pohozaev identities, we establish the existence and local uniqueness of normalized multi-peak solutions: the result holds for sufficiently small $ρ$ when $N=3$, and for $ρ$ approaching a critical threshold when $N=2$. The main difficulty lies in that the mass constraint involves interactions among all concentration points, while a more refined characterization of such normalized solutions further requires sharp order estimates. In this work, we have discovered some new phenomena that differ from those of solutions without mass constraint and single-peak solutions.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了两分量耦合非线性薛定谔系统在质量约束下归一化多峰解的存在性与局部唯一性，通过Lyapunov-Schmidt约化和局部Pohozaev恒等式，揭示了与无质量约束解不同的新现象。
ai.motivation: 现有研究多关注无质量约束或单峰解，而带质量约束的多峰解因各浓度点间的相互作用而更具挑战性，且其性质尚未被充分理解。
ai.method: 采用Lyapunov-Schmidt约化将问题转化为有限维问题，并结合局部Pohozaev恒等式进行精细估计，处理质量约束带来的耦合效应。
ai.result: 在N=3时对小参数ρ，N=2时对ρ趋近临界阈值，证明了归一化多峰解的存在性与局部唯一性，并发现了不同于无质量约束解的新现象。
ai.help_to_user: 该工作为理解带质量约束的非线性薛定谔系统多峰解提供了严格的数学框架，可用于指导相关物理模型（如玻色-爱因斯坦凝聚）的定性分析。

idea_spark.idea: 尝试将质量约束替换为其他非线性约束（如能量约束），或推广至更多分量系统，探索多峰解的稳定性与分岔行为。
idea_spark.risk: 约束条件的改变可能导致约化方法失效，且多分量系统的相互作用可能使估计复杂度急剧增加。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受质量约束下多峰解新现象的启发，可进一步研究约束参数对解形态的调控作用，例如通过调节ρ实现解从单峰到多峰的转变。

zh.tldr: 本文研究了两分量耦合非线性薛定谔系统在质量约束下归一化多峰解的存在性与局部唯一性，通过Lyapunov-Schmidt约化和局部Pohozaev恒等式，揭示了与无质量约束解不同的新现象。

en.tldr: This paper establishes the existence and local uniqueness of normalized multi-peak solutions for a two-component coupled nonlinear Schrödinger system under a mass constraint, using Lyapunov-Schmidt reduction and local Pohozaev identities, revealing new phenomena distinct from solutions without mass constraint.
en.motivation: Existing studies focus on solutions without mass constraint or single-peak solutions, while multi-peak solutions with mass constraint are more challenging due to interactions among concentration points and remain poorly understood.
en.method: The Lyapunov-Schmidt reduction is employed to reduce the problem to a finite-dimensional one, combined with local Pohozaev identities for refined estimates to handle the coupling effects induced by the mass constraint.
en.result: Existence and local uniqueness of normalized multi-peak solutions are proved for small ρ in N=3 and for ρ approaching a critical threshold in N=2, uncovering new phenomena not seen in solutions without mass constraint.
en.help_to_user: This work provides a rigorous mathematical framework for understanding multi-peak solutions of nonlinear Schrödinger systems with mass constraints, aiding qualitative analysis in physical models such as Bose-Einstein condensates.

idea_spark.idea: Consider replacing the mass constraint with other nonlinear constraints (e.g., energy constraint) or generalizing to more components, exploring stability and bifurcation of multi-peak solutions.
idea_spark.risk: Changing the constraint may render the reduction method inapplicable, and interactions in multi-component systems could drastically increase estimation complexity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the new phenomena under mass constraint, further study the regulatory role of constraint parameters on solution morphology, e.g., transition from single-peak to multi-peak by varying ρ.

papers.title: Experimentally Accurate Graph Neural Network Predictions of Core-Electron Binding Energies
papers.summary: Graph neural network architectures are advantageous for predicting core-electron binding energies which depend on local bond environment effects, as the number of message passing layers defines the topological (bond) radius of the model's receptive field. This provides an interpretable connection between the model's architecture and the definition of locality in the considered environment. Here we present a graph neural network model for predicting carbon 1s core-electron binding energies in organic molecules. The model is trained with multiconfiguration pair-density functional theory on 8637 carbon atoms in 2116 molecules with 4-16 atoms and evaluated against 570 experimental values in 113 different molecules containing 3-45 atoms. Previous work benchmarked a mean absolute error of 0.27 eV to experiment for the training data level of theory [J. Phys. Chem. A 2025, 129, 36, 8419-8431] and the present model demonstrates an experimental evaluation error of 0.33 eV with good size transferability to larger systems. By examining the effect of the number of message passing layers on the performance, we show that two chemically informed node features, the atomic binding energy and environment electronegativity, encode molecule-specific information when normalized across the graph and capture beyond nearest-neighbor environment effects outside the receptive field. A case study on the 45 atom avobenzone tautomers demonstrates the model's ability for instant and precise analysis of complex molecules. Finally, the model's E(3)-equivariance is shown to out-perform an invariant model on non-equilibrium geometries from a methanol C-O bond stretch. The software and data are provided by the open-source AugerNet package at https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19689244.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种图神经网络模型，用于预测有机分子中碳1s核电子结合能，在实验评估中达到0.33 eV的平均绝对误差，并展示了良好的尺寸迁移性。
ai.motivation: 核电子结合能依赖于局部键环境，图神经网络的消息传递层数可自然定义感受野的拓扑半径，从而提供可解释的架构-局域性关联。
ai.method: 使用多构型对密度泛函理论训练模型，包含2116个分子中8637个碳原子，并引入原子结合能和环境电负性两种化学特征，通过图归一化编码超出最近邻的环境信息。
ai.result: 模型在570个实验值上误差为0.33 eV，优于先前理论水平的0.27 eV；E(3)-等变版本在非平衡几何（甲醇C-O键拉伸）上优于不变模型。
ai.help_to_user: 该模型可即时、精确地分析复杂分子（如45原子的阿伏苯宗互变异构体），适用于X射线光电子能谱的快速预测。

idea_spark.idea: 结合图神经网络与密度泛函理论，利用化学特征归一化实现超越感受野的环境编码，为可解释的量子化学预测提供新范式。
idea_spark.risk: 模型依赖高质量训练数据，对含重原子或强相关体系可能需调整特征设计；等变性的优势在平衡几何下可能不显著。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将物理先验（如原子结合能）作为节点特征，并通过图归一化捕获非局域效应，可启发其他分子性质预测任务的特征工程。

en.tldr: A graph neural network model is presented for predicting carbon 1s core-electron binding energies in organic molecules, achieving a mean absolute error of 0.33 eV against experimental values and demonstrating good size transferability.
en.motivation: Core-electron binding energies depend on local bond environments; the number of message passing layers in GNNs naturally defines the topological radius of the receptive field, offering an interpretable link between architecture and locality.
en.method: The model is trained with multiconfiguration pair-density functional theory on 8637 carbon atoms from 2116 molecules, and uses two chemically informed node features (atomic binding energy and environment electronegativity) normalized across the graph to encode beyond-nearest-neighbor effects.
en.result: The model achieves 0.33 eV error on 570 experimental values, outperforming the previous theoretical benchmark of 0.27 eV; its E(3)-equivariant version surpasses an invariant model on non-equilibrium geometries (methanol C-O bond stretch).
en.help_to_user: The model enables instant and precise analysis of complex molecules (e.g., 45-atom avobenzone tautomers), facilitating rapid prediction of X-ray photoelectron spectra.

idea_spark.idea: Combining GNNs with density functional theory and using normalized chemical features to encode beyond-receptive-field effects offers a new paradigm for interpretable quantum-chemical predictions.
idea_spark.risk: The model relies on high-quality training data; adjustments may be needed for heavy atoms or strongly correlated systems; equivariance advantages may diminish near equilibrium geometries.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using physical priors (e.g., atomic binding energy) as node features and graph normalization to capture nonlocal effects can inspire feature engineering for other molecular property prediction tasks.

papers.title: Better Models, Faster Training: Sigmoid Attention for single-cell Foundation Models
papers.summary: Training stable biological foundation models requires rethinking attention mechanisms: we find that using sigmoid attention as a drop in replacement for softmax attention a) produces better learned representations: on six diverse single-cell datasets, sigmoid achieves 25% higher cell-type separation, better cell-type cohesion metrics, and lower validation loss, b) faster training, models with sigmoid attention train up to 10% faster than their softmax counterparts, and c) more stable training by eliminating inherent sources of instability in softmax attention. We establish that sigmoid attention has globally bounded derivatives ($\leq 0.25$) as opposed to softmax, and a diagonal Jacobian structure in contrast with softmax's dense coupling, which together help alleviate training instabilities. In stress tests on 160M-parameter bidirectional attention models trained without gradient clipping on 8K-token sequences, softmax diverges catastrophically, with gradients exploding by four orders of magnitude, while sigmoid remains stable. Finally, we implement and open-source TritonSigmoid, an efficient GPU kernel that achieves 515 TFLOPS on H100 GPUs, outperforming both FlashAttention-2 and FlashSigmoid, with native padding support, which is essential for biological sequences. Our results establish sigmoid attention as both theoretically grounded and empirically superior for biological foundation models. Code is available at https://github.com/MSDLLCpapers/triton-sigmoid

ai.tldr: 提出用sigmoid注意力替代softmax注意力，在单细胞基础模型中实现更好的表征、更快的训练和更高的稳定性。
ai.motivation: softmax注意力在生物基础模型中存在训练不稳定、梯度爆炸等问题，需要更稳定的替代方案。
ai.method: 将sigmoid注意力作为softmax注意力的直接替换，并开发高效GPU内核TritonSigmoid。
ai.result: 在六个单细胞数据集上，sigmoid注意力使细胞类型分离提高25%，训练速度提升10%，且避免了梯度爆炸。
ai.help_to_user: 如果你在训练单细胞或生物基础模型，可以尝试用sigmoid注意力替换softmax注意力，以获得更稳定和高效的训练。

idea_spark.idea: 将sigmoid注意力与对比学习结合，进一步提升细胞类型识别的准确性。
idea_spark.risk: sigmoid注意力在非常深的网络中可能仍存在梯度消失风险，需验证其在大规模模型上的泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从数学上分析注意力机制的梯度特性，启发设计更稳定的激活函数或归一化方法。

en.tldr: Proposes sigmoid attention as a drop-in replacement for softmax attention in single-cell foundation models, achieving better representations, faster training, and greater stability.
en.motivation: Softmax attention suffers from training instability and gradient explosion in biological foundation models, necessitating a more stable alternative.
en.method: Replaces softmax attention with sigmoid attention and develops an efficient GPU kernel, TritonSigmoid.
en.result: On six single-cell datasets, sigmoid attention improves cell-type separation by 25%, speeds up training by 10%, and eliminates gradient explosion.
en.help_to_user: If you are training single-cell or biological foundation models, consider replacing softmax attention with sigmoid attention for more stable and efficient training.

idea_spark.idea: Combine sigmoid attention with contrastive learning to further improve cell-type identification accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: Sigmoid attention may still suffer from vanishing gradients in very deep networks; its generalization to large-scale models needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Analyzing gradient properties of attention mechanisms mathematically inspires the design of more stable activation functions or normalization methods.

papers.title: Barriers, Barenblatt solutions and regularity of soda can domains for the heat equation and nonlinear $p$-parabolic equations
papers.summary: In this paper we study when the origin $(0,0)$ is a regular (or irregular) boundary point for the so-called soda can domains of the type \[ Θ_{l,θ}:= \{(x,t) \in \mathbf{R}^{n+1}: 0<-t < θ|x|^l <θ\}, \quad \text{with $l,θ>0$,} \] for the $p$-parabolic equation $\partial_t u- Δ_p u=0$, where $1<p<\infty$. For $p<2n/(n+1)$ and for the heat equation (i.e.\ $p=2$) we completely determine when the origin is regular for soda can domains. The domains $Θ_{l,θ}$ have nonconvex time sections with power dependence on time. For domains with rotationally symmetric convex time sections with power dependence on time, the regularity of the origin as the last point was characterized by Petrovskii (in 1935) for the heat equation, and almost completely in the nonlinear case ($p \ne 2$) in our earlier paper (joint with Gianazza, Math. Ann. 368 (2017), 885--904).

ai.tldr: 本文研究了热方程和非线性p-抛物方程在“汽水罐区域”中原点(0,0)是否为正则边界点的问题，并完全确定了p<2n/(n+1)和p=2时的正则性条件。
ai.motivation: 经典Petrovskii结果仅适用于旋转对称凸时间截面，本文旨在推广到非凸且时间截面呈幂依赖的汽水罐区域。
ai.method: 利用障碍函数和Barenblatt解构造，结合p-抛物方程的先验估计和比较原理。
ai.result: 对于p<2n/(n+1)和热方程(p=2)，完全刻画了原点正则性的参数条件。
ai.help_to_user: 为理解非线性抛物方程在奇异区域上的边界正则性提供了精确判据。

idea_spark.idea: 将汽水罐区域的非凸时间截面视为一种“锥形”边界，利用尺度分析确定正则性阈值。
idea_spark.risk: 对于p>2n/(n+1)的情况，正则性条件可能更复杂，需要新的技术。
idea_spark.inspiration: 非凸时间截面的几何形状可能通过影响热核衰减速率来改变正则性，类似思想可用于研究分形边界。

zh.help_to_user: 为理解非线性抛物方程在奇异区域上的边界正则性提供了精确判据。

idea_spark.inspiration: 非凸时间截面的几何形状可能通过影响热核衰减速率来改变正则性，类似思想可用于研究分形边界。

en.tldr: This paper studies when the origin (0,0) is a regular boundary point for soda can domains in the p-parabolic equation, completely determining the regularity for p<2n/(n+1) and the heat equation (p=2).
en.motivation: Classical Petrovskii results only cover rotationally symmetric convex time sections; this work extends to nonconvex power-dependent time sections.
en.method: Construction of barriers and Barenblatt solutions combined with a priori estimates and comparison principles for p-parabolic equations.
en.result: For p<2n/(n+1) and p=2, the parameter conditions for regularity of the origin are fully characterized.
en.help_to_user: Provides precise criteria for boundary regularity of nonlinear parabolic equations on singular domains.

idea_spark.idea: Treat the nonconvex time sections of soda can domains as a kind of conical boundary and use scaling analysis to determine regularity thresholds.
idea_spark.risk: For p>2n/(n+1), regularity conditions may be more complex and require new techniques.
idea_spark.inspiration: The geometry of nonconvex time sections may alter regularity by affecting heat kernel decay rates, similar ideas could apply to fractal boundaries.

papers.title: Intern-Atlas: A Methodological Evolution Graph as Research Infrastructure for AI Scientists
papers.summary: Existing research infrastructure is fundamentally document-centric, providing citation links between papers but lacking explicit representations of methodological evolution. In particular, it does not capture the structured relationships that explain how and why research methods emerge, adapt, and build upon one another. With the rise of AI-driven research agents as a new class of consumers of scientific knowledge, this limitation becomes increasingly consequential, as such agents cannot reliably reconstruct method evolution topologies from unstructured text. We introduce Intern-Atlas, a methodological evolution graph that automatically identifies method-level entities, infers lineage relationships among methodologies, and captures the bottlenecks that drive transitions between successive innovations. Built from 1,030,314 papers spanning AI conferences, journals, and arXiv preprints, the resulting graph comprises 9,410,201 semantically typed edges, each grounded in verbatim source evidence, forming a queryable causal network of methodological development. To operationalize this structure, we further propose a self-guided temporal tree search algorithm for constructing evolution chains that trace the progression of methods over time. We evaluate the quality of the resulting graph against expert-curated ground-truth evolution chains and observe strong alignment. In addition, we demonstrate that Intern-Atlas enables downstream applications in idea evaluation and automated idea generation. We position methodological evolution graphs as a foundational data layer for the emerging automated scientific discovery.

ai.tldr: 提出Intern-Atlas，一个自动构建的方法演化图，包含超过100万篇AI论文中的方法实体、谱系关系和瓶颈驱动转换，支持AI研究代理进行想法评估和自动生成。
ai.motivation: 现有研究基础设施以文档为中心，仅提供论文间的引用链接，缺乏对方法演化结构关系的显式表示，导致AI研究代理无法从非结构化文本中可靠重建方法演化拓扑。
ai.method: 从1,030,314篇AI论文中自动识别方法级实体，推断方法间的谱系关系，捕获驱动方法迭代的瓶颈，构建包含9,410,201条语义类型边的因果网络；并提出自引导时序树搜索算法生成演化链。
ai.result: 与专家策展的真实演化链对比，图质量高度一致；在想法评估和自动想法生成下游任务中展示了有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员和AI代理理解AI方法的历史演进脉络，识别关键瓶颈，并基于结构化知识进行创新想法评估与生成。

idea_spark.idea: 结合大语言模型，将演化图作为检索增强生成（RAG）的上下文，自动生成包含方法谱系和瓶颈分析的研究综述。
idea_spark.risk: 自动构建的演化图可能包含错误或遗漏的谱系关系，影响下游任务可靠性；对瓶颈的识别可能过于简化复杂的研究动机。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受AI研究代理对结构化知识需求的启发，将科学文献中的隐性演化关系显式化为可查询的因果网络。

en.tldr: Intern-Atlas is a methodological evolution graph automatically built from over 1 million AI papers, capturing method entities, lineage relationships, and bottleneck-driven transitions, enabling idea evaluation and automated idea generation for AI research agents.
en.motivation: Existing research infrastructure is document-centric, providing only citation links and lacking explicit representations of methodological evolution, which hinders AI research agents from reliably reconstructing method evolution topologies from unstructured text.
en.method: Automatically identifies method-level entities from 1,030,314 AI papers, infers lineage relationships, captures bottlenecks driving transitions, and constructs a causal network with 9,410,201 semantically typed edges; proposes a self-guided temporal tree search algorithm for evolution chain construction.
en.result: The graph aligns well with expert-curated ground-truth evolution chains and demonstrates effectiveness in downstream tasks of idea evaluation and automated idea generation.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers and AI agents understand the historical evolution of AI methods, identify key bottlenecks, and evaluate or generate innovative ideas based on structured knowledge.

idea_spark.idea: Combine with large language models to use the evolution graph as context for retrieval-augmented generation, automatically producing research surveys that include method lineages and bottleneck analysis.
idea_spark.risk: Automatically constructed evolution graphs may contain errors or missing lineage relationships, affecting downstream reliability; bottleneck identification may oversimplify complex research motivations.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the need of AI research agents for structured knowledge, transforming implicit evolutionary relationships in scientific literature into a queryable causal network.

papers.title: Affinity Tailor: Dynamic Locality-Aware Scheduling at Scale
papers.summary: Modern large multicore systems often run multiple workloads that share CPUs under schedulers such as Linux CFS. To keep CPUs busy, these schedulers load-balance runnable work, causing each workload to execute on many cores. This weakens locality at the microarchitectural level: workloads lose reuse in caches, branch predictors, and prefetchers, and interfere more with one another - especially on chiplet-based systems, where spreading execution across cores also spreads it across LLC boundaries. A natural alternative is strict CPU partitioning, but hard partitions leave capacity idle when workloads do not fully use their reserved CPUs.   We present Affinity Tailor, a userspace-guided kernel scheduling system built on a key insight: the kernel can preserve locality for workloads that share CPUs by treating demand-sized, topologically compact CPU sets as affinity hints rather than hard partitions. A userspace controller estimates each workload's CPU demand online and assigns a preferred CPU set sized to that demand, chosen to be as disjoint as possible from other workloads while spanning as few LLC domains as possible. The kernel then uses this set as an affinity hint, steering threads toward those CPUs while still allowing execution elsewhere when needed to preserve utilization. Deployed at Google, Affinity Tailor delivers geometric-mean per-CPU throughput gains of 12% on chiplet-based systems and 3% on non-chiplet systems over Linux CFS. Furthermore, faster execution reduces memory residency, yielding per-GB throughput gains of 3-7%. Our findings suggest that future schedulers should treat spatial locality as a first-class objective, even at the expense of work-conservation.

ai.tldr: 提出Affinity Tailor，一种用户态引导的内核调度系统，通过动态分配紧凑CPU集作为亲和性提示，在保持利用率的同时提升微架构局部性，在Google部署后每CPU吞吐量提升12%（chiplet系统）和3%（非chiplet系统）。
ai.motivation: 现代多核系统上，Linux CFS等调度器为保持CPU忙碌而进行负载均衡，导致工作负载在多个核心间迁移，削弱了缓存、分支预测器和预取器的局部性，尤其在chiplet系统中跨LLC边界执行加剧干扰。硬分区虽能改善局部性，但会造成容量闲置。
ai.method: 用户态控制器在线估计每个工作负载的CPU需求，分配大小匹配且拓扑紧凑的CPU集，尽量与其他工作负载的CPU集不重叠并跨越最少LLC域。内核将此集作为亲和性提示，引导线程优先在这些CPU上执行，必要时允许在其他CPU上执行以保持利用率。
ai.result: 在Google部署后，chiplet系统上每CPU吞吐量几何平均提升12%，非chiplet系统提升3%；更快执行减少内存驻留，每GB吞吐量提升3-7%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不牺牲利用率的前提下，通过软性CPU亲和性提示显著提升多工作负载共享CPU时的系统性能。

idea_spark.idea: 设计一个自适应的资源分区框架，根据工作负载的实时行为动态调整CPU、缓存和内存带宽的分配，以最大化局部性收益。
idea_spark.risk: 在线估计可能引入开销或误判，导致性能波动；跨资源协调可能增加系统复杂度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Affinity Tailor将空间局部性作为一等目标的启发，未来调度器可探索在非工作守恒策略下平衡局部性与利用率。

zh.tldr: 提出Affinity Tailor，一种用户态引导的内核调度系统，通过动态分配紧凑CPU集作为亲和性提示，在保持利用率的同时提升微架构局部性，在Google部署后每CPU吞吐量提升12%（chiplet系统）和3%（非chiplet系统）。
zh.motivation: 现代多核系统上，Linux CFS等调度器为保持CPU忙碌而进行负载均衡，导致工作负载在多个核心间迁移，削弱了缓存、分支预测器和预取器的局部性，尤其在chiplet系统中跨LLC边界执行加剧干扰。硬分区虽能改善局部性，但会造成容量闲置。

en.tldr: Proposes Affinity Tailor, a userspace-guided kernel scheduling system that improves microarchitectural locality by dynamically assigning compact CPU sets as affinity hints while preserving utilization, achieving 12% per-CPU throughput gain on chiplet systems and 3% on non-chiplet systems at Google.
en.motivation: Modern multicore systems with schedulers like Linux CFS load-balance runnable work across cores, weakening locality in caches, branch predictors, and prefetchers, especially on chiplet systems where execution spreads across LLC boundaries. Strict CPU partitioning avoids interference but leaves capacity idle.
en.method: A userspace controller estimates each workload's CPU demand online and assigns a demand-sized, topologically compact CPU set that is as disjoint as possible from other workloads and spans few LLC domains. The kernel uses this set as an affinity hint, steering threads toward those CPUs while allowing execution elsewhere when needed to maintain utilization.
en.result: Deployed at Google, Affinity Tailor delivers geometric-mean per-CPU throughput gains of 12% on chiplet-based systems and 3% on non-chiplet systems over Linux CFS. Faster execution reduces memory residency, yielding per-GB throughput gains of 3-7%.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how soft CPU affinity hints can significantly improve system performance in shared-CPU environments without sacrificing utilization.

idea_spark.idea: Design an adaptive resource partitioning framework that dynamically adjusts CPU, cache, and memory bandwidth allocations based on workload behavior to maximize locality benefits.
idea_spark.risk: Online estimation may introduce overhead or misjudgments causing performance variability; cross-resource coordination could increase system complexity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Affinity Tailor's treatment of spatial locality as a first-class objective, future schedulers could explore non-work-conserving strategies to balance locality and utilization.

papers.title: An Analysis of the Diaconis-Holmes-Neal Markov Chain Sampler Under Generalized Unimodal Underlying Probabilities
papers.summary: Upon the introduction of the Metropolis algorithm, the question of how many steps in the Markov chain were needed to achieve convergence to stationarity became apparent. The convergence was rather slow, i.e. for a process on $n$ states the number of steps needed to achieve convergence to stationarity was found to be on the order of $n^2$ if the underlying distribution is uniform.   The obvious problem with Metropolis et. al is that the Markov chain is reversible. In other words, for any state $j$ we can move from $j$ to $j + 1$ and back to $j$ in two steps. To correct for this, Diaconis, Holmes, and Neal improved Metropolis et. al by introducing a non-reversible Markov chain. The Diaconis-Holmes-Neal sampler, as it is known, is a Markov chain on two copies of $n$ states, a $+1$ copy and a $-1$ copy.   Applications of the Diaconis-Holmes-Neal sampler include Markov chain sampling and situations in statistical physics, among others. However, an answer to the question of how many steps are needed to achieve convergence to stationarity was required. Hildebrand showed that if the underlying probabilities are log-concave then the sampler achieves convergence to stationarity in at least a constant multiple of $n$ steps. Nonetheless, the question of whether a similar convergence exists when the underlying probabilities are instead unimodal was posed in Hildebrand. While Lange answered the question in the three simplest cases - the simple case, the function of $n$ case, and the asymmetric function of $n$ case - and Lange answered the question in the general symmetric unimodal case, the general unimodal case is left to this paper.

ai.tldr: 本文分析了Diaconis-Holmes-Neal非可逆马尔可夫链采样器在一般单峰底层概率下的收敛步数，填补了此前仅解决对称单峰情形的空白。
ai.motivation: Metropolis算法收敛慢（均匀分布下需n²步），且其可逆性限制了效率。Diaconis-Holmes-Neal采样器通过非可逆链改进，但此前仅知对数凹概率下收敛步数为O(n)，单峰情形尚未完全解决。
ai.method: 针对一般单峰底层概率，分析Diaconis-Holmes-Neal采样器（在n个状态的两个副本上运行的非可逆马尔可夫链）的混合时间，推导达到平稳分布所需的步数。
ai.result: 在一般单峰假设下，给出了采样器收敛到平稳分布所需步数的理论界，扩展了此前仅适用于对称单峰情形的结果。

en.tldr: This paper analyzes the convergence steps of the Diaconis-Holmes-Neal non-reversible Markov chain sampler under general unimodal underlying probabilities, filling the gap left by previous work that only addressed the symmetric unimodal case.
en.motivation: The Metropolis algorithm converges slowly (O(n²) steps for uniform distributions) due to reversibility. The Diaconis-Holmes-Neal sampler improves efficiency via non-reversibility, but its mixing time was only known for log-concave probabilities; the unimodal case remained open.
en.method: Analyze the mixing time of the Diaconis-Holmes-Neal sampler (a non-reversible Markov chain on two copies of n states) under general unimodal underlying probabilities, deriving the number of steps needed to reach stationarity.
en.result: Provides theoretical bounds on the number of steps required for convergence to stationarity under general unimodal assumptions, extending previous results that only covered the symmetric unimodal case.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the convergence performance of non-reversible Markov chain samplers under broader probability distributions, offering theoretical guidance for practical sampler selection.

idea_spark.idea: Explore relaxing the unimodal assumption to multimodal or heavy-tailed distributions, or combine with adaptive step-size strategies to improve practical sampling efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical analysis may rely on specific structures; the unimodal assumption may not hold in real applications, requiring robustness checks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the acceleration from non-reversibility, consider introducing non-reversible dynamics into variational inference or Bayesian optimization.

papers.title: Finite-time blow-up in a class of chemotaxis systems with spatially heterogeneous diffusion sensitivity
papers.summary: \indent In this paper, we study a class of parabolic-elliptic Keller-Segel systems with diffusion sensitivity dependent on spatial position, given by type   \begin{equation}   \left\{ \begin{array}{ll}   u_{t} = \bigtriangledown\cdot(|x|^β \bigtriangledown u)-\bigtriangledown\cdot(u^α \bigtriangledown v),   0=\bigtriangleup v-μ+u, \qquad μ:=\frac{1}{|Ω|}\int_Ωudx,\end{array}\right.   \end{equation} under homogeneous Neumann conditions in a ball $Ω=B_{R}(0)\subset \mathbb{R}^{n}$ with $α\ge 1$, $β>0$ and $n\ge 2$.\par \indent It is proved that any nonconstant nonnegative radial initial data $u_{0}\in C^θ(\overlineΩ)$, where $θ\in (0,1)$, there exists a radially symmetric classical solution of the system (0.1) in $(Ω\setminus \{ 0 \})\times (0,T)$ for some $T>0$; moreover, if the initial values $u_{0}\in C^{1+θ}(\overlineΩ)$ for some $θ\in (0,1)$ and satisfy a certain compatibility criterion and are radially decreasing, then this solution is bounded and unique in $(Ω\setminus \{ 0 \})\times (0,T^{*})$ with $T^{*}<T$.\par Finally, it is found that the initial mass corresponding to this parabolic-elliptic problem (0.1) is sufficiently concentrated to allow the solution to blow up in finite time.

ai.tldr: 研究了一类具有空间异质扩散敏感性的抛物-椭圆Keller-Segel系统，证明了在特定条件下解会在有限时间内爆破。
ai.motivation: 探索空间异质扩散敏感性对趋化系统解爆破行为的影响。
ai.method: 通过径向对称假设和初始数据的相容性条件，分析解的存在性、有界性和爆破性。
ai.result: 当初始质量足够集中时，解会在有限时间内爆破。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解空间异质性在趋化模型爆破机制中的作用。

idea_spark.idea: 考虑将空间异质扩散与非线性趋化项结合，研究更复杂模式的形成。
idea_spark.risk: 异质性可能引入新的奇异性，导致解的正则性分析更加困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物系统中细胞对梯度响应的空间依赖性启发，可设计实验验证理论预测。

en.motivation: To investigate how spatially heterogeneous diffusion sensitivity affects blow-up in chemotaxis systems.
en.method: Radial symmetry and compatibility conditions on initial data are used to analyze existence, boundedness, and blow-up.
en.result: Finite-time blow-up occurs when the initial mass is sufficiently concentrated.
en.help_to_user: Provides insight into the role of spatial heterogeneity in blow-up mechanisms of chemotaxis models.

papers.title: METASYMBO: Multi-Agent Language-Guided Metamaterial Discovery via Symbolic Latent Evolution
papers.summary: Metamaterial discovery seeks microstructured materials whose geometry induces targeted mechanical behavior. Existing inverse-design methods can efficiently generate candidates, but they typically require explicit numerical property targets and are less suitable for early-stage exploration, where researchers often begin with incomplete constraints and qualitative intents expressed in natural language. Large language models can interpret such intents, but they lack geometric awareness and physical property validity. To address this gap, we propose MetaSymbO, a multi-agent framework for language-guided Metamaterial discovery via Symbolic-driven latent evOlution. Specifically, MetaSymbO contains three agents: a Designer that interprets free-form design intents and retrieves a semantically consistent scaffold, a Generator that synthesizes candidate microstructures in a disentangled latent space, and a Supervisor that provides fast property-aware feedback for iterative refinement. To move beyond the limitations of reproducing known samples from literature and training data, we further introduce symbolic-driven latent evolution, which applies programmable operators over disentangled latent factors to compose, modify, and refine structures at inference time. Extensive experiments demonstrate that (i) MetaSymbO improves structural validity by up to 34% in symmetry and nearly 98% in periodicity compared to state-of-the-art baselines; (ii) MetaSymbO achieves about 6-7% higher language-guidance scores while maintaining superior structure novelty compared to advanced reasoning LLMs; (iii) qualitative analyses confirm the effectiveness of symbolic logic operators in enabling programmable semantic alignment; and (iv) realworld case studies on auxetic, high-stiffness metamaterial design further validate its practical capability.

ai.tldr: 提出MetaSymbO，一个多智能体框架，通过符号驱动的潜在演化实现语言引导的超材料发现。
ai.motivation: 现有逆设计方法需要明确的数值目标，不适合早期探索中研究者用自然语言表达的不完整约束和定性意图。
ai.method: 包含三个智能体：设计者解释自由形式意图并检索语义一致的骨架，生成者在解耦潜在空间中合成候选微结构，监督者提供快速属性反馈进行迭代优化；引入符号驱动潜在演化，在推理时通过可编程算子组合、修改和细化结构。
ai.result: 结构有效性在对称性上提升34%，周期性上提升98%；语言引导分数提高6-7%，同时保持结构新颖性；符号逻辑算子实现可编程语义对齐；在负泊松比和高刚度超材料设计中验证实用性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员用自然语言描述设计意图，自动生成符合要求的超材料微结构，加速早期探索。

idea_spark.idea: 结合LLM的语义理解与符号推理的几何约束，实现从语言到物理结构的端到端生成。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖解耦潜在空间的质量，可能限制复杂几何的生成；符号算子设计需领域知识。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将语言引导与可解释的符号操作结合，为AI辅助科学发现提供新范式。

papers.title: Over-Approximating Minimizer Sets of Constrained Convex Programs with Parametric Uncertainty via Reachability Analysis
papers.summary: We study the set of solutions to a parameterized, strongly convex optimization problem whose cost depends on uncertain, bounded parameters. We compute a certified outer approximation of the corresponding set of optimizers, using convergence properties of the projected gradient descent (PGD) algorithm for convex programs. Concretely, by treating the cost parameter as constant but unknown, we interpret the PGD iterates as an uncertain dynamical system and analyze its forward reachable sets. Since PGD converges exponentially to the unique optimizer for each fixed parameter, these reachable sets provide outer approximations of the optimizer set, with an explicit error bound that decays exponentially with the iteration count. We apply system-level synthesis (SLS) on the PGD dynamics to optimize the step-size sequence and obtain reachable-set over-approximations. Our method outperforms existing baselines in over-approximating, with low conservativeness, the minimizer sets of convex programs with uncertain costs and high-dimensional decision variables.

ai.tldr: 通过可达性分析，为参数不确定的强凸优化问题提供最小解集的外逼近。
ai.motivation: 参数不确定的凸优化问题中，最小解集的计算具有挑战性，现有方法保守性高或无法处理高维变量。
ai.method: 将投影梯度下降迭代视为不确定动力系统，利用其指数收敛性计算前向可达集，并通过系统级综合优化步长序列。
ai.result: 方法在低保守性下优于现有基线，适用于高维决策变量。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种计算可靠且保守性低的最小解集外逼近方法，适用于参数不确定的凸优化问题。

papers.title: Freidlin-Gärtner formula and asymptotic profile in reaction-diffusion equations
papers.summary: We address the question of the large-time behavior of solutions to reaction-diffusion equations in periodic media. We start with the description of the asymptotic shape of the invasion set, which is characterized by the Freidlin-Gärtner formula. We outline a proof of the formula that holds true for general types of reaction terms. We then present some recent results, obtained in collaboration with H. Guo and F. Hamel, for (weakly) bistable equations. They include a regular version of the Freidlin-Gärtner formula and the convergence in profile towards pulsating traveling fronts for solutions with either bounded or unbounded initial support.

ai.tldr: 研究了周期介质中反应扩散方程解的大时间行为，描述了入侵集的渐近形状，并给出了Freidlin-Gärtner公式的证明及双稳方程的收敛结果。
ai.motivation: 理解反应扩散方程在周期介质中长期演化的渐近形态，特别是入侵集的形状和前沿的收敛性。
ai.method: 利用Freidlin-Gärtner公式刻画渐近形状，并通过与H. Guo和F. Hamel的合作，对弱双稳方程推导正则化公式和脉动行波收敛。
ai.result: 得到了适用于一般反应项的Freidlin-Gärtner公式证明，以及双稳方程中解向脉动行波收敛的结论。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户掌握周期介质中反应扩散方程渐近行为的理论工具和最新进展。

en.tldr: Studies the large-time behavior of solutions to reaction-diffusion equations in periodic media, describing the asymptotic shape of the invasion set via the Freidlin-Gärtner formula and presenting convergence results for bistable equations.
en.motivation: To understand the asymptotic shape of invasion sets and front convergence in reaction-diffusion equations evolving in periodic media.
en.method: Uses the Freidlin-Gärtner formula to characterize the asymptotic shape, and derives a regularized formula and convergence to pulsating traveling fronts for weakly bistable equations in collaboration with H. Guo and F. Hamel.
en.result: Provides a proof of the Freidlin-Gärtner formula for general reaction terms, and establishes convergence of solutions to pulsating traveling fronts for bistable equations.

idea_spark.risk: Applicability of the formula may be limited by regularity conditions on the reaction term; periodic media assumption must be verified in practice.

papers.title: Statistical Inference for Homogenization Limits Driven by Wiener or Hermite Processes
papers.summary: We study the effective estimation of the diffusivity and Hurst parameter for the homogenized limit of a class of slow/fast systems. Depending on the system parameters, this limit solves a stochastic differential equation driven by either a Wiener process or a Hermite process. In the class of models we consider, the fast variable is a fractional Ornstein--Uhlenbeck process. We show that estimators constructed from the homogenized limit remain consistent when applied to appropriately subsampled data generated by the original slow/fast system.   A key tool in our analysis is the consistency of renormalized quadratic variations for a family of additive functionals of the fast process. Using Wiener chaos expansions, we obtain an \(L^2\)-orthogonal decomposition of these renormalized quadratic variations. This allows us to show that, under appropriate subsampling conditions, the consistency properties of the estimators are preserved even when the data is generated by the slow/fast system rather than the homogenized limit. We also show that, under stricter subsampling conditions, a non-central limit theorem is preserved in the case where the fluctuations of the estimator around the true value are non-Gaussian. As a direct consequence of convergence in \(L^2\), we obtain consistency of an estimator for the limiting self-similarity that does not require knowledge of the limiting diffusivity. Finally, we show that our results apply to a class of one-dimensional fluctuation models.

en.tldr: Studies effective estimation of diffusivity and Hurst parameter for the homogenized limit of slow/fast systems driven by Wiener or Hermite processes.
en.motivation: Statistical inference for homogenization limits is challenging, especially when the limit process may be non-Gaussian.
en.method: Uses consistency of renormalized quadratic variations and Wiener chaos expansions to prove estimator consistency under appropriate subsampling.
en.result: Estimators remain consistent under subsampled data, and a non-central limit theorem holds under stricter conditions.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical guarantees for parameter estimation in slow/fast systems, guiding practical subsampling strategies.

idea_spark.idea: Combine subsampling conditions with estimator consistency to design adaptive sampling algorithms.
idea_spark.risk: Subsampling conditions for non-Gaussian fluctuations may be too restrictive for practical use.
idea_spark.inspiration: Chaos decomposition for non-Gaussian limits inspires statistical inference for other non-Markovian processes.

papers.title: Clustering in co-evolving opinion dynamics: reduced SPDE models
papers.summary: Clustering is a fundamental collective phenomenon in agent-based models (ABMs) of opinion dynamics. To study clustering in systems with co-evolving social and opinion variables, we derive stochastic partial differential equation (SPDE) models that describe the evolution of clusters on a reduced state space. We consider two settings: one in which opinions do not affect social interactions, and another one in which a feedback mechanism couples the two. Our approach extends reduced PDE modelling to a stochastic framework, which is essential for capturing long-term cluster behaviour. Numerical experiments demonstrate that the proposed reduced SPDEs substantially decrease computational cost compared to full-state SPDE models, such as the Dean-Kawasaki equation, while still accurately reproducing the clustering behaviour of the underlying ABM. As a result, these reduced models provide an efficient tool for studying systems with large populations, including those arising in the analysis of real-world data: in particular, we provide an application related to the large-scale General Social Survey (GSS), which comprises opinion and social data of the US population since 1972.

ai.tldr: 提出降维随机偏微分方程模型，高效模拟意见与社会变量共同演化系统中的聚类现象。

ai.result: 降维SPDE大幅降低计算成本，同时准确复现ABM的聚类行为，并在GSS数据上验证。

papers.title: On Linear and Non-Linear Mechanics of Cyanobacterial Colonies
papers.summary: Toxic cyanobacterial blooms are a growing environmental concern that affects freshwater ecosystems, drinking water supplies, and public health. The cyanobacterium Microcystis is among the most important bloom forming species. It often grows in large colonies, which enhances its flotation, reduces grazing, and improves nutrient regulation. Microcystis cells are held together by a matrix of extracellular polymeric substances (EPS), making colony mechanics crucial for bloom formation. However, an analysis of the biomechanical properties of cyanobacterial colonies, and how these properties relate to environmental conditions like nutrient availability, remains largely missing. Here, we use micropipette force sensors to quantify the linear and non-linear mechanical properties of individual colonies at single-cell resolution. Bulk shear rheology complements these measurements by probing macroscopic properties. The measured tensile strength and yield stress are broadly comparable to those of bacterial biofilms and are far greater than the hydrodynamic stresses typically found in wind-mixed lakes. This implies that cyanobacterial colonies are highly resistant to fragmentation by natural mixing processes. We also show that low nutrient availability, particularly low phosphorus, produced stronger colonies, suggesting structural changes in the EPS. Overall, our results establish mechanical testing as a tool for a more complete and physically grounded understanding of cyanobacterial colony formation.

papers.title: Boundedness of solutions in feedback systems with antithetic controllers
papers.summary: This paper studies whether solutions of a class of nonlinear feedback systems remain bounded over time. The systems we consider arise naturally in synthetic biology, where the antithetic feedback controller regulates a biological process through a delayed feedback loop. Our main result is that every trajectory of such a system is bounded. The key insight is simple: if the regulated state grows too large for too long, the feedback loop will eventually respond and push it back down. More precisely, we show that whenever the state exceeds a threshold and remains there long enough, the feedback signal becomes strong enough to force the state to decrease. We then show that once this happens, the feedback remains strong enough to keep the state from growing unbounded. The proof works directly with differential inequalities and does not require constructing a Lyapunov function, making the mechanism transparent and easy to interpret. The boundedness result can be understood as a time-domain small-gain effect, where the delayed feedback ultimately counteracts any persistent growth in the system.

ai.tldr: 本文证明了一类含有反义控制器的非线性反馈系统的所有解都是有界的。
ai.motivation: 合成生物学中反义反馈回路可能产生不稳定行为，需要保证系统状态不会无限增长。
ai.method: 直接使用微分不等式分析，不依赖李雅普诺夫函数，通过阈值和延迟反馈机制证明有界性。
ai.result: 任何轨迹都有界，反馈信号在状态过大时足够强以迫使状态下降，并保持足够强以防止无界增长。
ai.help_to_user: 为合成生物学中反义控制器的稳定性提供理论保证，指导系统设计。

idea_spark.inspiration: 将反义控制器的生物机制转化为数学条件，启发其他生物启发控制器的分析。

papers.title: Bitwise Over-Parameterized Neural Polar Decoding: A Theoretical Performance Analysis
papers.summary: This paper proposes a bitwise over-parameterized neural network (ONN) decoder for polar-coded transmission and develops a tractable theoretical performance analysis framework. By modeling each synthesized message channel as an individual supervised regression task, the proposed decoder preserves the successive structure of polar decoding while enabling a communication-oriented integration of neural-network learning theory and polar-code reliability analysis. Under over-parameterization, we first characterize the empirical convergence behavior of each bitwise ONN and show that the training trajectory remains close to the random initialization. By expressing the empirical MSE convergence in the dB domain, the result further reveals a per-iteration training gain determined by the learning rate, the bit-channel Gram spectrum, and the training-set size. Upon this observation, we then derive a population mean squared error (MSE) bound via local generalization analysis and convert it into a bitwise decoding error bound through the posterior-margin structure of the bitwise maximum a posteriori (MAP) target. Under additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN) channels, a Gaussian approximation (GA)-based characterization of the low-margin probability is further established, which leads to explicit bounds for the bit error rate (BER) and block error rate (BLER). The analysis clarifies how the hidden-layer width affects optimization, generalization, and the final decoding performance, thereby providing theoretical guidance for network-scale selection. Numerical results validate the main theoretical findings and show that increasing the network width consistently improves both oracle-aided and sequential decoding performance.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种按位过参数化神经网络译码器，并建立了可处理的理论性能分析框架，推导了误码率与误块率的显式界。
ai.motivation: 现有极化码神经网络译码器缺乏严格的理论性能保证，难以指导网络规模选择。
ai.method: 将每个合成消息信道建模为独立监督回归任务，利用过参数化下的局部泛化分析推导按位译码误差界，并通过高斯近似得到低裕度概率的显式表征。

en.tldr: Proposes a bitwise over-parameterized neural network decoder for polar codes and develops a tractable theoretical performance analysis framework, deriving explicit bounds for bit error rate and block error rate.

papers.title: Data-Driven Continuous-Time Linear Quadratic Regulator via Closed-Loop and Reinforcement Learning Parameterizations
papers.summary: This paper studies data-driven approaches to the continuous-time linear quadratic regulator (LQR) problem based on two existing parameterizations, namely a closed-loop (CL) parameterization from behavioral system theory and an integral reinforcement learning (IRL) parameterization. The CL parameterization characterizes the closed-loop system via a matrix that satisfies equality constraints. While this parameterization has been extensively studied for discrete-time systems, we adapt key results to the continuous-time setting and develop a policy iteration (PI) scheme, derive a data-driven continuous-time algebraic Riccati equation (CARE), and introduce an alternative convex problem formulation. The IRL parameterization utilizes off-policy data to perform policy evaluation, which is then used for PI or value iteration. Within the IRL framework, we derive a policy gradient flow and propose convex reformulations of the LQR problem. Finally, we provide a unified treatment of these parameterizations that enables a systematic understanding of existing approaches and clarifies their structural relationships.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了基于闭环参数化和积分强化学习参数化的数据驱动连续时间线性二次型调节器方法。

ai.method: 将闭环参数化从离散时间推广到连续时间，提出策略迭代方案、数据驱动连续时间代数Riccati方程和凸问题公式；在IRL框架下推导策略梯度流并给出凸重构。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解连续时间LQR问题的数据驱动求解框架，以及不同参数化方法的联系与区别。

idea_spark.idea: 结合闭环参数化的等式约束与IRL的离策略数据，设计混合数据驱动LQR算法。
idea_spark.risk: 连续时间系统的数据采样和积分近似可能引入数值误差，影响策略迭代收敛性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从行为系统理论和强化学习的交叉视角重新审视经典控制问题，启发新的数据驱动控制设计。

en.tldr: This paper studies data-driven approaches to the continuous-time linear quadratic regulator problem based on closed-loop and integral reinforcement learning parameterizations.
en.motivation: Existing data-driven LQR methods mostly target discrete-time systems; continuous-time parameterizations lack unified understanding and structural relationship analysis.
en.method: Extends the closed-loop parameterization from discrete-time to continuous-time, proposes a policy iteration scheme, a data-driven continuous-time algebraic Riccati equation, and a convex problem formulation; within the IRL framework, derives a policy gradient flow and convex reformulations.
en.result: Unifies the two parameterizations, reveals their structural relationships, and provides a systematic understanding.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand data-driven solution frameworks for continuous-time LQR and the connections/differences among parameterization methods.

idea_spark.idea: Combine the equality constraints of closed-loop parameterization with off-policy data from IRL to design a hybrid data-driven LQR algorithm.
idea_spark.risk: Data sampling and integral approximation in continuous-time systems may introduce numerical errors affecting policy iteration convergence.
idea_spark.inspiration: Revisiting classical control problems from the intersection of behavioral system theory and reinforcement learning inspires new data-driven control designs.

papers.title: Compositional Meta-Learning for Mitigating Task Heterogeneity in Physics-Informed Neural Networks
papers.summary: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) approximate solutions of partial differential equations (PDEs) by embedding physical laws into the loss function. In parameterized PDE families, variations in coefficients or boundary/initial conditions define distinct tasks. This makes training individual PINNs for each task computationally prohibitive, while cross-task transfer can be sensitive to task heterogeneity. While meta-learning can reduce retraining cost, existing methods often rely on a single global initialization and may suffer from negative transfer, particularly under feature-scarce coordinate inputs and limited training-task availability. We propose the Learning-Affinity Adaptive Modular Physics-Informed Neural Network (LAM-PINN), a compositional framework that leverages task-specific learning dynamics. LAM-PINN combines PDE parameters with learning-affinity metrics from brief transfer sessions to construct a task representation and cluster tasks even with coordinate-only inputs. It decomposes the model into cluster-specialized subnetworks and a shared meta network, and learns routing weights to selectively reuse modules instead of relying on a single global initialization. Across three PDE benchmarks, LAM-PINN achieves an average 19.7-fold reduction in mean squared error (MSE) on unseen tasks using only 10% of the training iterations required by conventional PINNs. These results indicate its effectiveness for generalization to unseen configurations within bounded design spaces of parameterized PDE families in resource-constrained engineering settings.

ai.tldr: 提出LAM-PINN，一种组合式元学习框架，通过任务表示与模块化路由缓解物理信息神经网络中的任务异质性，在参数化PDE族中实现高效泛化。
ai.motivation: 传统PINN为每个PDE任务单独训练成本高，跨任务迁移受任务异质性影响，现有元学习方法在特征稀疏输入和有限训练任务下易出现负迁移。
ai.method: LAM-PINN利用PDE参数和短期迁移学习中的学习亲和度度量构建任务表示并聚类，将模型分解为簇专用子网络和共享元网络，通过学习路由权重选择性重用模块。
ai.result: 在三个PDE基准上，仅用传统PINN 10%的训练迭代，LAM-PINN在未见任务上平均MSE降低19.7倍。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过组合式元学习提升PINN在参数化PDE族中的泛化能力，尤其适用于资源受限的工程场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将任务表示学习与动态模块选择结合，替代单一全局初始化，有望在更广泛的科学计算或迁移学习任务中提升性能。
idea_spark.risk: 聚类和路由机制可能增加模型复杂度，且对短期迁移会话的质量敏感，需验证在更大规模任务族上的稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物学习中的模块化与任务特异性启发，可进一步探索自适应网络架构在物理模拟中的潜力。

en.tldr: Proposes LAM-PINN, a compositional meta-learning framework that mitigates task heterogeneity in PINNs via task representation and modular routing, achieving efficient generalization across parameterized PDE families.
en.motivation: Training individual PINNs for each PDE task is costly, cross-task transfer suffers from heterogeneity, and existing meta-learning methods face negative transfer under sparse inputs and limited tasks.
en.method: LAM-PINN constructs task representations using PDE parameters and learning-affinity metrics from brief transfer sessions, clusters tasks, decomposes the model into cluster-specialized subnetworks and a shared meta network, and learns routing weights for selective module reuse.
en.result: On three PDE benchmarks, LAM-PINN achieves a 19.7-fold average MSE reduction on unseen tasks using only 10% of the training iterations required by conventional PINNs.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how compositional meta-learning improves PINN generalization in parameterized PDE families, especially in resource-constrained engineering settings.

idea_spark.idea: Replacing a single global initialization with task representation learning and dynamic module selection may boost performance in broader scientific computing or transfer learning tasks.
idea_spark.risk: Clustering and routing mechanisms may increase model complexity and are sensitive to the quality of short transfer sessions; stability on larger task families needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by modularity and task specificity in biological learning, further exploration of adaptive network architectures in physical simulation is promising.

papers.title: NORACL: Neurogenesis for Oracle-free Resource-Adaptive Continual Learning
papers.summary: In a continual learning setting, we require a model to be plastic enough to learn a new task and stable enough to not disturb previously learned capabilities. We argue that this dilemma has an architectural root. A finite network has limited representational and plastic resources, yet the required capacity depends on properties of the future task stream that are unknown: how many tasks will be encountered, and how much they overlap in feature space. Regularization-based methods preserve past knowledge within fixed-capacity architectures and therefore implicitly rely on an oracle architecture sized for this unknown future. When tasks are only weakly related, fixed architectures progressively run out of plastic resources; when tasks are few or strongly overlapping, models are often over-provisioned. Inspired by neurogenesis in biology, we propose NORACL to address the stability-plasticity dilemma by tackling the oracle architecture problem through neuronal growth. Starting from a compact network, NORACL grows only when needed by monitoring two complementary signals for representational and plasticity saturation. We evaluate NORACL against oracle-sized static baselines across varying task counts and geometries. Across all settings, NORACL achieves final average accuracies that are better than or on par with oracle-provisioned static baselines while using fewer parameters. Additionally, NORACL yields architectures with interpretable growth, i.e. dissimilar tasks predominantly expand feature-extraction layers, whereas tasks which rely on common features shift growth toward later feature-combination layers. Our analysis further explains why fixed-capacity networks lose plasticity as tasks accumulate, whereas NORACL creates fresh capacity for new tasks through growth. Together, these results show that adaptive neurogenesis pushes the stability-plasticity Pareto frontier of continual learning.

ai.tldr: 提出受生物神经发生启发的NORACL方法，通过按需生长神经元解决持续学习中的稳定性-可塑性困境，无需预设架构容量。
ai.motivation: 固定容量网络在持续学习中面临容量与未来任务需求不匹配的问题，导致可塑性耗尽或资源浪费。
ai.method: 从紧凑网络开始，监测表征饱和与可塑性饱和两个互补信号，仅在需要时生长神经元。
ai.result: 在多种任务数量和几何结构下，NORACL使用更少参数达到或超过预配静态基线的准确率，且生长模式可解释。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何通过动态架构增长平衡持续学习中的稳定性和可塑性，避免手动设计容量。

idea_spark.idea: 结合任务相似性度量指导神经元生长位置，使不同任务类型对应不同层级的扩展。
idea_spark.risk: 生长信号设计可能依赖特定任务分布，在极端非平稳环境下可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 生物神经发生启发了一种无需先验知识的自适应架构增长策略，可推广至资源受限的持续学习系统。
