idea_spark.idea: 将高斯原语与视觉语言模型结合，实现无需训练的开放词汇3D占据预测。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖SLAM精度和视觉语言模型的质量，在复杂动态场景中可能退化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用现成模型（SLAM、VLM）组合解决传统需要大量标注的任务，启发无监督3D感知新范式。

en.tldr: FreeOcc is a training-free open-vocabulary occupancy prediction framework that builds a globally consistent occupancy map from monocular or RGB-D sequences using SLAM, 3D Gaussian maps, and vision-language models, without 3D annotations or ground-truth camera poses.
en.motivation: Existing learning-based occupancy prediction methods rely on large-scale 3D annotations and generalize poorly across environments.
en.method: A four-layer pipeline: SLAM estimates poses and sparse geometry; geometrically consistent Gaussian update constructs dense 3D Gaussian maps; open-vocabulary semantics from off-the-shelf VLMs are associated with Gaussian primitives; probabilistic Gaussian-to-occupancy projection produces dense voxel occupancy.
en.result: Achieves over 2× improvements in IoU and mIoU on EmbodiedOcc-ScanNet compared to prior self-supervised methods, and transfers zero-shot to novel environments, outperforming both supervised and self-supervised baselines.
en.help_to_user: Provides a training-free, annotation-free occupancy prediction solution suitable for robot navigation and scene understanding.

idea_spark.idea: Combine Gaussian primitives with vision-language models to enable training-free open-vocabulary 3D occupancy prediction.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on SLAM accuracy and VLM quality, which may degrade in complex dynamic scenes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging off-the-shelf models (SLAM, VLM) to solve traditionally annotation-heavy tasks inspires a new paradigm for unsupervised 3D perception.

papers.title: Anchored Peskin Problem
papers.summary: The Immersed Boundary Method has long served as a robust computational framework for fluid-structure interactions, yet the rigorous analysis of 1D Peskin filaments anchored to rigid boundaries remains sparse. In this paper, we generalize the classical Peskin problem to the half-plane by considering an elastic filament whose endpoints are anchored to a no-slip wall. Moving beyond the algebraic complexity of the traditional Blake image system, we utilize the boundary-symmetric formulation of Gimbutas, Greengard, and Veerapaneni. This representation allows for a transparent decomposition of the hydrodynamic interactions into a free space principal part and a regularizing reflected component without resorting to hypersingular integral operators. Through this framework, we prove that the leading-order evolution of the anchored filament is governed by a fractional Laplacian equipped with homogeneous Dirichlet boundary conditions. We characterize the stationary states of the system, proving that all equilibria are circular arcs connecting the anchor points, a result that holds for a broad class of elastic energy densities. By framing the non-local dynamics in weighted little Hölder spaces, we establish local well posedness and prove that the filament exhibits instantaneous $C^\infty$ regularization in both space and time. This work provides a rigorous analytical foundation for anchored filaments in bounded domains and suggests a spectrally accurate numerical path for simulating tethered biological structures.

ai.tldr: 将经典Peskin问题推广到半平面，分析端点锚定在无滑移壁上的弹性细丝，证明其主导演化由带齐次Dirichlet边界条件的分数阶Laplacian控制，平衡态为连接锚点的圆弧，并建立局部适定性和瞬时光滑性。
ai.motivation: 浸入边界法广泛应用于流固耦合，但一维Peskin细丝锚定于刚性边界的严格分析尚属空白。
ai.method: 采用Gimbutas-Greengard-Veerapaneni的边界对称公式，将水动力相互作用分解为自由空间主部和正则化反射分量，避免超奇异积分算子。
ai.result: 细丝主导演化由分数阶Laplacian控制；所有平衡态为连接锚点的圆弧；在加权little Hölder空间中建立局部适定性和时空瞬时C∞正则化。
ai.help_to_user: 为锚定细丝在有界域中的分析提供严格基础，并提示模拟系留生物结构的谱精确数值路径。

idea_spark.idea: 利用谱方法数值实现该分数阶Laplacian模型，模拟纤毛或细胞骨架细丝在壁面附近的动力学。
idea_spark.risk: 分数阶Laplacian的数值离散可能引入额外误差，且实际生物细丝可能具有非线性弹性或主动力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将经典Peskin问题与边界积分方法结合，为复杂几何中的流固耦合提供解析可处理框架。

zh.tldr: 将经典Peskin问题推广到半平面，分析端点锚定在无滑移壁上的弹性细丝，证明其主导演化由带齐次Dirichlet边界条件的分数阶Laplacian控制，平衡态为连接锚点的圆弧，并建立局部适定性和瞬时光滑性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用谱方法数值实现该分数阶Laplacian模型，模拟纤毛或细胞骨架细丝在壁面附近的动力学。
idea_spark.risk: 分数阶Laplacian的数值离散可能引入额外误差，且实际生物细丝可能具有非线性弹性或主动力。

en.tldr: Generalizes the classical Peskin problem to the half-plane by analyzing an elastic filament anchored to a no-slip wall, proving its leading-order evolution is governed by a fractional Laplacian with homogeneous Dirichlet boundary conditions, equilibria are circular arcs, and establishing local well-posedness with instantaneous C∞ regularization.
en.motivation: The Immersed Boundary Method is widely used for fluid-structure interactions, but rigorous analysis of 1D Peskin filaments anchored to rigid boundaries is sparse.
en.method: Uses the boundary-symmetric formulation of Gimbutas, Greengard, and Veerapaneni to decompose hydrodynamic interactions into a free-space principal part and a regularizing reflected component, avoiding hypersingular integral operators.
en.result: Leading-order evolution governed by a fractional Laplacian; all equilibria are circular arcs connecting anchor points; local well-posedness and instantaneous C∞ regularization in space and time in weighted little Hölder spaces.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous analytical foundation for anchored filaments in bounded domains and suggests a spectrally accurate numerical path for simulating tethered biological structures.

idea_spark.idea: Implement a spectral numerical method for this fractional Laplacian model to simulate the dynamics of cilia or cytoskeletal filaments near walls.
idea_spark.risk: Numerical discretization of the fractional Laplacian may introduce additional errors, and real biological filaments may have nonlinear elasticity or active forces.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining the classical Peskin problem with boundary integral methods provides an analytically tractable framework for fluid-structure interactions in complex geometries.

papers.title: Binary Spiking Neural Networks as Causal Models
papers.summary: We provide a causal analysis of Binary Spiking Neural Networks (BSNNs) to explain their behavior. We formally define a BSNN and represent its spiking activity as a binary causal model. Thanks to this causal representation, we are able to explain the output of the network by leveraging logic-based methods. In particular, we show that we can successfully use a SAT as well as a SMT solver to compute abductive explanations from this binary causal model. To illustrate our approach, we trained the BSNN on the standard MNIST dataset and applied our SAT-based and SMT-based methods to finding abductive explanations of the network's classifications based on pixel-level features. We also compared the found explanations against SHAP, a popular method used in the area of explainable AI. We show that, unlike SHAP, our approach guarantees that a found explanation does not contain completely irrelevant features.

ai.tldr: 将二元脉冲神经网络（BSNN）建模为因果模型，利用SAT/SMT求解器生成基于像素特征的溯因解释，并证明其解释不含无关特征。
ai.motivation: 现有可解释AI方法（如SHAP）可能包含无关特征，需要一种能保证解释简洁且因果相关的方法。
ai.method: 将BSNN的脉冲活动形式化为二元因果模型，使用SAT和SMT求解器计算溯因解释。
ai.result: 在MNIST数据集上，该方法生成的解释不含无关特征，优于SHAP。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解BSNN分类决策的因果逻辑，提供更可靠的特征归因。

idea_spark.idea: 将SAT/SMT求解器用于神经网络的因果解释，替代传统特征重要性方法。
idea_spark.risk: 计算复杂度可能随网络规模增加，需优化求解效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合形式化方法与神经网络可解释性，开辟新的解释路径。

zh.tldr: 将二元脉冲神经网络（BSNN）建模为因果模型，利用SAT/SMT求解器生成基于像素特征的溯因解释，并证明其解释不含无关特征。
zh.motivation: 现有可解释AI方法（如SHAP）可能包含无关特征，需要一种能保证解释简洁且因果相关的方法。
zh.method: 将BSNN的脉冲活动形式化为二元因果模型，使用SAT和SMT求解器计算溯因解释。
zh.result: 在MNIST数据集上，该方法生成的解释不含无关特征，优于SHAP。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解BSNN分类决策的因果逻辑，提供更可靠的特征归因。

idea_spark.idea: 将SAT/SMT求解器用于神经网络的因果解释，替代传统特征重要性方法。
idea_spark.risk: 计算复杂度可能随网络规模增加，需优化求解效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合形式化方法与神经网络可解释性，开辟新的解释路径。

en.tldr: Models Binary Spiking Neural Networks as causal models, uses SAT/SMT solvers to generate pixel-level abductive explanations, and guarantees no irrelevant features. Outperforms SHAP on MNIST.
en.motivation: Existing explainable AI methods like SHAP may include irrelevant features; need a method that guarantees concise and causally relevant explanations.
en.method: Formalizes BSNN spiking activity as a binary causal model, then uses SAT and SMT solvers to compute abductive explanations.
en.result: On MNIST, the generated explanations contain no irrelevant features, outperforming SHAP.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the causal logic behind BSNN classifications, providing more reliable feature attribution.

idea_spark.idea: Use SAT/SMT solvers for neural network causal explanations, replacing traditional feature importance methods.
idea_spark.risk: Computational complexity may scale with network size; solver efficiency needs optimization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining formal methods with neural network interpretability opens new explanation pathways.

papers.title: In-Context Prompting Obsoletes Agent Orchestration for Procedural Tasks
papers.summary: Agent orchestration frameworks -- LangGraph, CrewAI, Google ADK, OpenAI Agents SDK, and others -- place an external orchestrator above the LLM, tracking state and injecting routing instructions at every turn. We present a controlled comparison showing that for procedural tasks, this architecture is dominated by a simpler alternative: putting the entire procedure in the system prompt and letting the model self-orchestrate. Across three domains -- travel booking (14 nodes), Zoom technical support (14 nodes), and insurance claims processing (55 nodes) -- we evaluate 200 conversations per condition using LLM-as-judge scoring on five quality criteria. The in-context approach scores 4.53--5.00 on a 5-point scale while a LangGraph orchestrator using the same model scores 4.17--4.84. The orchestrated system fails on 24% of travel, 9% of Zoom, and 17% of insurance conversations, compared to 11.5%, 0.5%, and 5% for the in-context baseline. While external orchestration may have been necessary for earlier models, advances in frontier model capabilities have made it unnecessary for multi-turn conversations following a defined procedure.

ai.tldr: 对于程序性任务，将完整流程放入系统提示词让模型自我编排，效果优于外部编排框架。
ai.motivation: 现有代理编排框架在每一步都依赖外部编排器跟踪状态和注入路由指令，增加了复杂性和失败率。
ai.method: 在旅行预订、Zoom技术支持、保险理赔三个领域，用LLM作为裁判对200次对话进行5项质量评分，比较上下文提示与LangGraph编排的效果。
ai.result: 上下文提示方法在5分制上得4.53-5.00，而LangGraph编排得4.17-4.84；编排系统在三个领域的失败率分别为24%、9%、17%，上下文提示仅为11.5%、0.5%、5%。
ai.help_to_user: 提示用户对于遵循固定流程的多轮对话任务，直接使用上下文提示即可，无需引入外部编排框架。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将更复杂的动态流程（如条件分支、循环）也通过上下文提示实现自我编排。
idea_spark.risk: 对于需要严格状态控制或安全约束的任务，完全依赖模型自我编排可能引入不可预测行为。
idea_spark.inspiration: 前沿模型能力的提升使得简化架构成为可能，启发我们重新审视传统AI系统的设计假设。

idea_spark.inspiration: 前沿模型能力的提升使得简化架构成为可能，启发我们重新审视传统AI系统的设计假设。

en.tldr: For procedural tasks, putting the entire procedure in the system prompt and letting the model self-orchestrate outperforms external orchestration frameworks.
en.motivation: Existing agent orchestration frameworks rely on an external orchestrator to track state and inject routing instructions at every step, increasing complexity and failure rates.
en.method: In three domains (travel booking, Zoom technical support, insurance claims processing), we compare in-context prompting with LangGraph orchestration using LLM-as-judge scoring on 200 conversations per condition across five quality criteria.
en.result: The in-context approach scores 4.53-5.00 on a 5-point scale, while LangGraph scores 4.17-4.84; orchestrated system fails on 24%, 9%, and 17% of conversations vs. 11.5%, 0.5%, and 5% for in-context.
en.help_to_user: Suggests that for multi-turn conversations following a defined procedure, in-context prompting suffices without external orchestration.

idea_spark.idea: Explore implementing more complex dynamic flows (e.g., conditional branches, loops) via in-context self-orchestration.
idea_spark.risk: For tasks requiring strict state control or safety constraints, relying solely on model self-orchestration may introduce unpredictable behavior.
idea_spark.inspiration: Advances in frontier model capabilities enable architecture simplification, inspiring a rethinking of traditional AI system design assumptions.

papers.title: Optimal Functional Incentives for Control: The Linear-Quadratic Case with Bilinear Incentives
papers.summary: We study the design of functional incentive mechanisms for dynamical systems, in which a leader designs a fixed incentive function to motivate a self-interested follower to actuate the system beneficially over an extended horizon, without real-time revision of the incentive. This stands in contrast to the adaptive paradigm, in which the incentive is itself a continuously updated control variable. We formalize the problem as a discrete-time bi-level optimal control problem and derive analytical results for the linear-quadratic case with bilinear incentives and a myopic follower. Specifically, we establish a necessary and sufficient stability condition for the induced closed-loop system, derive a closed-form expression for the gradient of the expected leader cost with respect to the incentive parameter matrix, and obtain a fully closed-form cost expression in the scalar setting. Based on the latter, explicit characterizations of the optimal incentive parameter are provided in two asymptotic regimes: the infinite-horizon limit and the limit of high follower cost. For long horizons, the optimal incentive is shown to become independent of the follower's private cost parameter, with direct implications for robust mechanism design under private information.

ai.tldr: 研究领导者设计固定激励函数以引导自私追随者长期有益地控制系统，在线性二次型与双线性激励情形下给出解析结果。
ai.motivation: 传统自适应激励需实时调整，而固定激励函数更简单且适用于无法实时通信的场景。
ai.method: 将问题形式化为离散时间双层最优控制，针对线性二次型系统、双线性激励和短视追随者推导稳定性条件、梯度闭式及标量情形下的完全闭式成本。
ai.result: 得到闭环系统稳定的充要条件、期望成本关于激励参数的梯度闭式，以及标量情形下最优激励参数在无限时域和高追随者成本极限下的显式刻画。
ai.help_to_user: 为设计无需实时调整的激励机制提供理论依据，尤其适用于领导者对追随者私有成本信息不确定的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将固定激励函数视为一种“承诺”机制，可结合鲁棒优化处理追随者模型不确定性。
idea_spark.risk: 假设追随者短视且系统线性二次型，实际非线性或前瞻性追随者可能使结论失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴机制设计中的“激励相容”思想，将控制问题转化为静态博弈设计问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将固定激励函数视为一种“承诺”机制，可结合鲁棒优化处理追随者模型不确定性。
idea_spark.risk: 假设追随者短视且系统线性二次型，实际非线性或前瞻性追随者可能使结论失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴机制设计中的“激励相容”思想，将控制问题转化为静态博弈设计问题。

en.tldr: Studies the design of fixed functional incentives for a leader to motivate a self-interested follower in dynamical systems, providing analytical results for the linear-quadratic case with bilinear incentives.
en.motivation: Adaptive incentives require real-time updates; fixed incentives are simpler and suitable when real-time communication is infeasible.
en.method: Formulates the problem as a discrete-time bi-level optimal control problem, derives stability conditions, gradient expressions, and closed-form costs for the scalar case.
en.result: Obtains necessary and sufficient stability condition, closed-form gradient of expected leader cost, and explicit optimal incentive characterizations in infinite-horizon and high-follower-cost limits.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical foundations for designing incentive mechanisms that do not require real-time adjustments, especially under private follower cost information.

idea_spark.idea: View fixed incentive functions as commitment devices; combine with robust optimization to handle model uncertainty about the follower.
idea_spark.risk: Assumes myopic follower and linear-quadratic dynamics; nonlinear or forward-looking followers may invalidate results.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrows from mechanism design's 'incentive compatibility' to reframe control as a static game design problem.

papers.title: Continuous-tone Simple Points: An $\ell_0$-Norm of Cyclic Gradient for Topology-Preserving Data-Driven Image Segmentation
papers.summary: Topological features play an essential role in ensuring geometric plausibility and structural consistency in image analysis tasks such as segmentation and skeletonization. However, integrating topology-preserving learning based on simple points into deep learning tasks remains challenging, as existing simple point detection methods are confined to binary images and are non-differentiable, rendering them incompatible with gradient-based optimization in modern deep learning. Moreover, morphological and purely data-driven approaches often fail to guaranty topological consistency. To address these limitations, we propose a novel method that directly computes simple points on continuous-valued images, enabling differentiable topological inference. Building on this theory, we develop an efficient skeleton extraction algorithm that preserves topological structures in binary and continuous-valued images. Furthermore, we design a variational model that enforces topological constraints by preserving topologically non-removable (i.e., non-simple) points, which can be seamlessly integrated into any deep neural network segmentation with softmax or sigmoid outputs. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively improves topological integrity and structural accuracy across multiple benchmarks. The codes are available in https://github.com/levnsio/CSP.

ai.tldr: 提出一种在连续值图像上直接计算简单点的方法，实现可微分的拓扑推理，并设计变分模型将拓扑约束无缝集成到深度神经网络中，提升分割的拓扑完整性。
ai.motivation: 现有简单点检测局限于二值图像且不可微，无法用于梯度优化的深度学习；形态学或纯数据驱动方法难以保证拓扑一致性。
ai.method: 定义连续色调简单点，基于循环梯度的ℓ0范数实现可微拓扑推理；开发高效骨架提取算法；构建变分模型保留拓扑不可移除点，可嵌入任意softmax/sigmoid输出的分割网络。
ai.result: 在多个基准上有效提升了拓扑完整性和结构准确性。
ai.help_to_user: 若您需要拓扑保持的图像分割或骨架提取，本方法可直接集成到现有深度学习框架中，无需修改网络结构。

idea_spark.idea: 将连续色调简单点与对抗训练结合，增强模型对拓扑扰动的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 连续值简单点的定义可能对噪声敏感，需验证在低信噪比图像上的稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受二值图像简单点理论启发，将其扩展到连续域，利用ℓ0范数实现可微性，思路可迁移到其他离散拓扑特征的连续化。

idea_spark.idea: 将连续色调简单点与对抗训练结合，增强模型对拓扑扰动的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 连续值简单点的定义可能对噪声敏感，需验证在低信噪比图像上的稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受二值图像简单点理论启发，将其扩展到连续域，利用ℓ0范数实现可微性，思路可迁移到其他离散拓扑特征的连续化。

en.tldr: Proposes a method to compute simple points directly on continuous-valued images, enabling differentiable topological inference, and designs a variational model to integrate topological constraints into deep neural networks, improving topological integrity in segmentation.
en.motivation: Existing simple point detection is limited to binary images and non-differentiable, incompatible with gradient-based deep learning; morphological or data-driven methods often fail to guarantee topological consistency.
en.method: Defines continuous-tone simple points via ℓ0-norm of cyclic gradient for differentiable inference; develops efficient skeleton extraction; builds a variational model preserving topologically non-removable points, seamlessly integrable into any segmentation network with softmax/sigmoid outputs.
en.result: Demonstrates improved topological integrity and structural accuracy across multiple benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: If you need topology-preserving image segmentation or skeleton extraction, this method can be directly integrated into existing deep learning frameworks without modifying network architecture.

idea_spark.idea: Combine continuous-tone simple points with adversarial training to enhance model robustness against topological perturbations.
idea_spark.risk: The definition of continuous-tone simple points may be sensitive to noise; stability on low-SNR images needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by simple point theory in binary images, extended to continuous domain via ℓ0-norm for differentiability; this approach can be transferred to continuous relaxation of other discrete topological features.

papers.title: Fibonacci numbers and the probability of polygon formation using random length sticks
papers.summary: We present two complementary proofs that, if the lengths of $n$ sticks are sampled at random, then the probability that no $p+1$ sticks can form a $(p+1)$-sided polygon can be expressed as the product of the reciprocals of a series of terms involving the $p$-step Fibonacci numbers. The first proof uses matrix algebra to extend the method previously used by Sudbury et al. to derive expressions for the probabilities of not being able to form triangles and quadrilaterals. The second alternative proof uses a different approach based on expressions for the minimum and maximum lengths of each stick that are compatible with the constraint of not being able to form a $(p+1)$-sided polygon, and provides insights into the structure of the probability expressions and the underlying reason that they include the Fibonacci numbers. Furthermore, the approach is developed in a generalised way that can, in principle, be applied to sticks randomly sampled from any probability distribution.

ai.tldr: 本文提出两种互补证明，表明随机长度棍子无法形成p+1边形的概率可表示为p步斐波那契数倒数乘积。
ai.motivation: 推广Sudbury等人关于三角形和四边形无法形成的概率表达式，并揭示斐波那契数出现的深层原因。
ai.method: 第一种证明使用矩阵代数扩展先前方法；第二种证明基于与无法形成多边形兼容的棍子最小和最大长度表达式，并推广到任意概率分布。
ai.result: 无法形成p+1边形的概率等于一系列包含p步斐波那契数的项的倒数乘积。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种计算随机长度棍子无法形成多边形概率的通用方法，并揭示了斐波那契数在几何概率中的自然出现。

idea_spark.idea: 利用斐波那契数倒数乘积形式，可设计快速算法估计随机棍子集合的多边形形成概率。
idea_spark.risk: 对于非均匀分布，表达式可能复杂，需验证收敛性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 斐波那契数在几何概率中的出现启发我们探索其他组合序列（如卢卡斯数）在类似问题中的角色。

zh.motivation: 推广Sudbury等人关于三角形和四边形无法形成的概率表达式，并揭示斐波那契数出现的深层原因。

zh.help_to_user: 提供了一种计算随机长度棍子无法形成多边形概率的通用方法，并揭示了斐波那契数在几何概率中的自然出现。

idea_spark.idea: 利用斐波那契数倒数乘积形式，可设计快速算法估计随机棍子集合的多边形形成概率。
idea_spark.risk: 对于非均匀分布，表达式可能复杂，需验证收敛性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 斐波那契数在几何概率中的出现启发我们探索其他组合序列（如卢卡斯数）在类似问题中的角色。

en.tldr: Two complementary proofs show that the probability that no p+1 sticks can form a (p+1)-sided polygon is the product of reciprocals of terms involving p-step Fibonacci numbers.
en.motivation: Generalize previous results for triangles and quadrilaterals, and uncover the structural reason for Fibonacci numbers appearing.
en.method: First proof uses matrix algebra to extend prior method; second proof uses expressions for minimum and maximum stick lengths compatible with the constraint, and generalizes to any probability distribution.
en.result: The probability of not forming a (p+1)-sided polygon equals the product of reciprocals of terms involving p-step Fibonacci numbers.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general method to compute the probability that random-length sticks cannot form a polygon, and reveals the natural emergence of Fibonacci numbers in geometric probability.

idea_spark.idea: Using the product-of-reciprocals form, one can design fast algorithms to estimate polygon formation probabilities for random stick sets.
idea_spark.risk: For non-uniform distributions, expressions may become complex and convergence needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: The appearance of Fibonacci numbers in geometric probability suggests exploring other combinatorial sequences (e.g., Lucas numbers) in similar problems.

papers.title: Frank-Wolfe Beyond 1/t Convergence
papers.summary: We consider smooth convex minimization over compact convex sets, i.e., $\min_{x \in C} f(x)$ with the (vanilla) Frank-Wolfe algorithm. Well-known lower bounds establish a worst-case $Ω(1/t)$ primal-gap barrier in the general smooth convex case, and faster convergence usually requires favorable function properties such as Hölder error bounds or strong convexity. We present a new Local Dual Sharpness (LDS) condition, essentially a property of the feasible region and its LMO, under which the Frank-Wolfe algorithm converges in $o(1/t)$ for any smooth convex function, ruling out an $Ω(1/t)$ lower bound under LDS. The condition is a generalization (and localization) of uniform convexity of sets and it is satisfied by any uniformly convex set. To our knowledge, this is the first unconditional $o(1/t)$ convergence result for uniformly convex sets. Combining LDS with stronger function properties, e.g., a local variant of Hölder error bounds, allows us to quantify the actual rates.

ai.tldr: 提出局部对偶尖锐性条件，证明Frank-Wolfe算法在光滑凸极小化中可突破1/t收敛下界。
ai.motivation: 经典下界表明Frank-Wolfe算法在一般光滑凸情形下收敛率为Ω(1/t)，突破此下界通常需要函数具有强凸性或Hölder误差界等额外性质。本文旨在寻找仅依赖可行域几何性质即可加速收敛的条件。
ai.method: 定义局部对偶尖锐性条件，该条件是集合一致凸性的推广与局部化，并证明在此条件下对任意光滑凸函数Frank-Wolfe算法达到o(1/t)收敛。
ai.result: 首次证明对于一致凸集，Frank-Wolfe算法无条件实现o(1/t)收敛；结合局部Hölder误差界可进一步量化具体速率。
ai.help_to_user: 为理解Frank-Wolfe算法在几何结构良好集合上的加速机制提供理论依据，指导算法设计与收敛性分析。

idea_spark.idea: 将集合的一致凸性局部化，并利用线性最小化预言机的几何信息推导收敛加速。
idea_spark.risk: 条件验证可能依赖于具体集合结构，实际应用中难以事先判断是否满足。

zh.tldr: 提出局部对偶尖锐性条件，证明Frank-Wolfe算法在光滑凸极小化中可突破1/t收敛下界。
zh.motivation: 经典下界表明Frank-Wolfe算法在一般光滑凸情形下收敛率为Ω(1/t)，突破此下界通常需要函数具有强凸性或Hölder误差界等额外性质。本文旨在寻找仅依赖可行域几何性质即可加速收敛的条件。

en.tldr: Proposes Local Dual Sharpness condition, proving Frank-Wolfe algorithm can break the 1/t convergence barrier for smooth convex minimization.
en.motivation: Classical lower bounds show Ω(1/t) convergence for Frank-Wolfe in general smooth convex case; breaking this barrier typically requires extra function properties like strong convexity or Hölder error bounds. This work seeks conditions solely on the geometry of the feasible set that enable faster convergence.
en.method: Defines Local Dual Sharpness condition, a generalization and localization of uniform convexity of sets, and proves that under this condition Frank-Wolfe achieves o(1/t) convergence for any smooth convex function.
en.result: First unconditional o(1/t) convergence result for uniformly convex sets; combining with local Hölder error bounds yields explicit rates.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical insight into acceleration mechanisms of Frank-Wolfe on well-structured sets, guiding algorithm design and convergence analysis.

idea_spark.idea: Localize uniform convexity of sets and exploit geometric information from the linear minimization oracle to derive convergence acceleration.
idea_spark.risk: Verification of the condition may depend on specific set structure, making it hard to check in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: Breaking algorithm lower bounds via set geometry rather than function properties inspires rethinking the interplay between geometry and algebra in optimization.

papers.title: AdvDMD: Adversarial Reward Meets DMD For High-Quality Few-Step Generation
papers.summary: Diffusion models offer superior generation quality at the expense of extensive sampling steps.   Distillation methods, with Distribution Matching Distillation (DMD) as a popular example, can mitigate this issue, but performance degradation remains pronounced when sampling steps are limited.   Reinforcement learning (RL) has been leveraged to improve the few-step generation quality during distillation, with the potential to even surpass the performance of the teacher model. However, existing approaches are combinatorial in nature, merely integrating an RL process with the distillation process, which introduces unnecessary complexities. To address this gap, we propose AdvDMD, a method that seamlessly unifies DMD distillation and RL.   Specifically, AdvDMD employs the adversarially trained discriminator from DMD2 as the reward model, which assigns low scores to generated images and high scores to real ones.   It is trained on both intermediate and final states of the denoising process and updated online with the distilled model, enabling a holistic supervision of the sampling trajectories and mitigating reward hacking.   We adopt a unified SDE backward simulation and a different training schedule for DMD and RL to enable a more stable and efficient training.   Experimental results demonstrate that the 4-step AdvDMD outperforms the original 40-step model for SD3.5 on DPG-Bench, while achieving significant performance gains for SD3 on the GenEval.   On Qwen-Image, our 2-step AdvDMD achieves superior performance over TwinFlow.

ai.tldr: 提出AdvDMD方法，将对抗奖励与分布匹配蒸馏统一，实现高质量少步生成。
ai.motivation: 现有蒸馏方法在少步采样时性能下降，而结合强化学习的方法复杂且易出现奖励破解。
ai.method: 利用DMD2的对抗判别器作为奖励模型，对去噪中间和最终状态进行在线训练，并采用统一SDE反向模拟和差异化训练调度。
ai.result: 4步AdvDMD在SD3.5上超越原40步模型，2步在Qwen-Image上优于TwinFlow。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过对抗奖励统一蒸馏与强化学习，提升少步生成质量。

idea_spark.idea: 将判别器同时用于蒸馏和强化学习奖励，减少额外组件。
idea_spark.risk: 对抗训练可能不稳定，需要精细调度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从DMD2的判别器设计出发，将其奖励功能扩展到强化学习阶段。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从DMD2的判别器设计出发，将其奖励功能扩展到强化学习阶段。

en.tldr: Proposes AdvDMD, unifying adversarial reward with distribution matching distillation for high-quality few-step generation.
en.motivation: Existing distillation methods degrade with few sampling steps, and RL integration is complex and prone to reward hacking.
en.method: Uses the adversarially trained discriminator from DMD2 as a reward model, trained online on intermediate and final denoising states, with unified SDE backward simulation and separate training schedules.
en.result: 4-step AdvDMD outperforms 40-step SD3.5 on DPG-Bench; 2-step AdvDMD surpasses TwinFlow on Qwen-Image.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how adversarial rewards unify distillation and RL to improve few-step generation quality.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage the same discriminator for both distillation and RL reward, reducing extra components.
idea_spark.risk: Adversarial training may be unstable and requires careful scheduling.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by DMD2's discriminator design, extending its reward function to the RL phase.

papers.title: ClipTBP: Clip-Pair based Temporal Boundary Prediction with Boundary-Aware Learning for Moment Retrieval
papers.summary: Video moment retrieval is the task of retrieving specific segments of a video corresponding to a given text query. Recent studies have been conducted to improve multimodal alignment performance through visual-linguistic similarity learning at the snippet-level and transformer-based temporal boundary regression. However, existing models do not calculate similarity by considering the relationships between multiple answer segments that match the query. Therefore, existing models are easily influenced by visually similar segments in the surrounding context. Existing models calculate similarity at the snippet-level and ignore the relationships between multiple answer segments corresponding to a single query. Therefore, they struggle to exclude segments irrelevant to the query. To address this issues, we propose ClipTBP, a clip-pair temporal boundary prediction framework based on boundary-aware learning. ClipTBP introduces a clip-level alignment loss for explicitly learning the semantic relationship between answer segments. ClipTBP also predicts accurate temporal boundaries by applying both main boundary loss and auxiliary boundary loss. ClipTBP consistently improves performance when applied to various existing models and demonstrates more robust boundary prediction performance even in ambiguous query scenarios.

ai.tldr: 提出ClipTBP框架，通过片段对时序边界预测和边界感知学习，提升视频时刻检索中多答案片段的关系建模与边界预测精度。
ai.motivation: 现有模型仅计算片段级相似度，忽略多个答案片段间的语义关系，易受视觉相似片段干扰，难以排除无关片段。
ai.method: 引入片段级对齐损失显式学习答案片段语义关系，并联合主边界损失与辅助边界损失预测精确时序边界。
ai.result: 在多种现有模型上持续提升性能，在模糊查询场景下表现出更鲁棒的边界预测能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户更准确地从视频中检索与文本查询匹配的特定片段，尤其在查询模糊时仍能保持高精度。

idea_spark.idea: 利用对比学习增强片段对间的语义一致性，并设计多任务损失联合优化边界预测。
idea_spark.risk: 片段对采样策略可能引入噪声，影响训练稳定性；辅助边界损失的超参数调节需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从多实例学习角度出发，将多个答案片段视为正例对，通过关系建模提升检索鲁棒性。

zh.tldr: 提出ClipTBP框架，通过片段对时序边界预测和边界感知学习，提升视频时刻检索中多答案片段的关系建模与边界预测精度。

idea_spark.idea: 利用对比学习增强片段对间的语义一致性，并设计多任务损失联合优化边界预测。
idea_spark.risk: 片段对采样策略可能引入噪声，影响训练稳定性；辅助边界损失的超参数调节需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从多实例学习角度出发，将多个答案片段视为正例对，通过关系建模提升检索鲁棒性。

en.tldr: Proposes ClipTBP, a clip-pair temporal boundary prediction framework with boundary-aware learning, to improve relationship modeling among multiple answer segments and boundary prediction accuracy in video moment retrieval.
en.motivation: Existing models compute snippet-level similarity ignoring relationships between multiple answer segments, making them susceptible to visually similar distractors and unable to exclude irrelevant segments.
en.method: Introduces a clip-level alignment loss to explicitly learn semantic relationships among answer segments, and jointly applies main and auxiliary boundary losses for precise temporal boundary prediction.
en.result: Consistently improves performance when applied to various existing models, and demonstrates more robust boundary prediction even under ambiguous queries.
en.help_to_user: Helps users retrieve specific video segments matching a text query more accurately, especially under ambiguous query conditions.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage contrastive learning to enhance semantic consistency among clip pairs, and design multi-task losses for joint boundary optimization.
idea_spark.risk: Clip-pair sampling may introduce noise affecting training stability; hyperparameters for auxiliary boundary loss require careful tuning.
idea_spark.inspiration: View multiple answer segments as positive pairs from a multi-instance learning perspective, improving retrieval robustness through relationship modeling.

papers.title: Gait Recognition via Deep Residual Networks and Multi-Branch Feature Fusion
papers.summary: Gait recognition has emerged as a compelling biometric modality for surveillance and security applications, offering inherent advantages such as non-intrusiveness, resistance to disguise, and long-range identification capability. However, prevailing approaches struggle to comprehensively capture and exploit the rich biometric cues embedded in human locomotion, particularly under covariate interference including viewpoint variation, clothing change, and carrying conditions. In this paper, we present a high-precision gait recognition framework that deeply extracts and synergistically fuses gait dynamics with body shape characteristics through a multi-branch architecture grounded in deep residual learning. Specifically, we first employ the High-Resolution Network (HRNet) to perform robust skeletal keypoint estimation, preserving fine-grained spatial information even under low-resolution inputs. We then construct three complementary feature branches -- body proportion, gait velocity, and skeletal motion -- from the extracted pose sequences. A 50-layer Residual Network (ResNet-50) backbone is leveraged within a deep feature extraction module to capture hierarchically rich and discriminative representations. To effectively integrate heterogeneous feature streams, we design a Multi-Branch Feature Fusion (MFF) module inspired by channel-wise attention mechanisms, which dynamically allocates contribution weights across branches through learned activation parameters. Extensive experiments on the cross-view multi-condition CASIA-B benchmark demonstrate that our method achieves a Rank-1 accuracy of 94.52\% under normal walking, with the best recognition performance among skeleton-based methods for the coat-wearing condition.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于深度残差网络和多分支特征融合的步态识别框架，在CASIA-B数据集上达到94.52%的Rank-1准确率。
ai.motivation: 现有步态识别方法难以全面捕捉人体运动中的丰富生物特征，尤其在视角、衣着和携带物等干扰下性能下降。
ai.method: 使用HRNet进行骨骼关键点估计，构建身体比例、步态速度和骨骼运动三个互补特征分支，通过ResNet-50提取深度特征，并设计基于通道注意力的多分支特征融合模块动态分配权重。
ai.result: 在CASIA-B数据集上，正常行走Rank-1准确率94.52%，穿外套条件下达到骨架方法最佳性能。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种高精度步态识别方案，适用于监控安防等非接触式身份识别场景。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将步态特征与人体其他生物特征（如步幅、姿态）进行跨模态融合，以进一步提升鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 对极端遮挡或低质量输入可能仍存在性能瓶颈，需验证在真实监控环境下的泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受HRNet高分辨率表示启发，可尝试将类似思路用于其他需要细粒度空间信息的任务，如手势识别。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于深度残差网络和多分支特征融合的步态识别框架，在CASIA-B数据集上达到94.52%的Rank-1准确率。
zh.motivation: 现有步态识别方法难以全面捕捉人体运动中的丰富生物特征，尤其在视角、衣着和携带物等干扰下性能下降。

zh.help_to_user: 提供了一种高精度步态识别方案，适用于监控安防等非接触式身份识别场景。

idea_spark.risk: 对极端遮挡或低质量输入可能仍存在性能瓶颈，需验证在真实监控环境下的泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受HRNet高分辨率表示启发，可尝试将类似思路用于其他需要细粒度空间信息的任务，如手势识别。

en.tldr: Proposes a gait recognition framework using deep residual networks and multi-branch feature fusion, achieving 94.52% Rank-1 accuracy on CASIA-B.
en.motivation: Existing gait recognition methods struggle to capture rich biometric cues under covariate interference like viewpoint, clothing, and carrying conditions.
en.method: Employs HRNet for skeletal keypoint estimation, constructs three complementary feature branches (body proportion, gait velocity, skeletal motion), uses ResNet-50 for deep feature extraction, and designs a channel-wise attention-based multi-branch feature fusion module.
en.result: Achieves 94.52% Rank-1 accuracy on CASIA-B under normal walking and best skeleton-based performance under coat-wearing condition.
en.help_to_user: Provides a high-precision gait recognition solution for non-intrusive identity verification in surveillance and security applications.

idea_spark.idea: Explore cross-modal fusion of gait features with other biometrics (e.g., stride, posture) to further improve robustness.
idea_spark.risk: May still face performance bottlenecks under extreme occlusion or low-quality input; generalization in real surveillance environments needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by HRNet's high-resolution representation, similar ideas could be applied to tasks requiring fine-grained spatial information, such as gesture recognition.

papers.title: Investigating More Explainable and Partition-Free Compositionality Estimation for LLMs: A Rule-Generation Perspective
papers.summary: Compositional generalization tests are often used to estimate the compositionality of LLMs. However, such tests have the following limitations: (1) they only focus on the output results without considering LLMs' understanding of sample compositionality, resulting in explainability defects; (2) they rely on dataset partition to form the test set with combinations unseen in the training set, suffering from combination leakage issues. In this work, we propose a novel rule-generation perspective for compositionality estimation for LLMs. It requires LLMs to generate a program as rules for dataset mapping and provides estimates of the compositionality of LLMs using complexity-based theory. The perspective addresses the limitations of compositional generalization tests and provides a new way to analyze the compositionality characterization of LLMs. We conduct experiments and analysis of existing advanced LLMs based on this perspective on a string-to-grid task, and find various compositionality characterizations and compositionality deficiencies exhibited by LLMs.

ai.tldr: 提出从规则生成角度估计大语言模型组合性的新方法，克服了传统组合泛化测试的可解释性差和组合泄露问题。
ai.motivation: 传统组合泛化测试仅关注输出结果，忽略模型对样本组合性的理解，且依赖数据集划分导致组合泄露，缺乏可解释性。
ai.method: 让LLM生成程序作为数据集映射的规则，并基于复杂度理论估计其组合性。
ai.result: 在字符串到网格任务上对现有先进LLM进行实验，发现了多种组合性特征和缺陷。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种更可解释、无需数据集划分的LLM组合性评估方法，帮助用户理解模型的内在组合能力。

idea_spark.idea: 利用复杂度理论量化LLM生成的规则，可能揭示模型在组合任务中的认知瓶颈。
idea_spark.risk: 规则生成任务本身可能引入新的偏差，且复杂度度量需谨慎设计以避免误导性结论。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考：是否可以用类似方法评估LLM对逻辑规则或因果结构的理解？

zh.tldr: 提出从规则生成角度估计大语言模型组合性的新方法，克服了传统组合泛化测试的可解释性差和组合泄露问题。
zh.motivation: 传统组合泛化测试仅关注输出结果，忽略模型对样本组合性的理解，且依赖数据集划分导致组合泄露，缺乏可解释性。
zh.method: 让LLM生成程序作为数据集映射的规则，并基于复杂度理论估计其组合性。
zh.result: 在字符串到网格任务上对现有先进LLM进行实验，发现了多种组合性特征和缺陷。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种更可解释、无需数据集划分的LLM组合性评估方法，帮助用户理解模型的内在组合能力。

idea_spark.idea: 利用复杂度理论量化LLM生成的规则，可能揭示模型在组合任务中的认知瓶颈。
idea_spark.risk: 规则生成任务本身可能引入新的偏差，且复杂度度量需谨慎设计以避免误导性结论。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考：是否可以用类似方法评估LLM对逻辑规则或因果结构的理解？

en.tldr: Proposes a rule-generation perspective for estimating compositionality in LLMs, overcoming explainability and combination leakage issues of traditional tests.
en.motivation: Traditional compositional generalization tests only focus on outputs, ignore models' understanding of compositionality, and suffer from combination leakage due to dataset partitioning.
en.method: Requires LLMs to generate programs as mapping rules and estimates compositionality using complexity-based theory.
en.result: Experiments on a string-to-grid task with advanced LLMs reveal diverse compositionality characteristics and deficiencies.
en.help_to_user: Offers a more explainable and partition-free method to evaluate LLM compositionality, aiding understanding of models' intrinsic compositional abilities.

idea_spark.idea: Using complexity theory to quantify LLM-generated rules may reveal cognitive bottlenecks in compositional tasks.
idea_spark.risk: Rule generation tasks may introduce new biases, and complexity metrics require careful design to avoid misleading conclusions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires thinking: can similar methods evaluate LLM understanding of logical rules or causal structures?

papers.title: Optimization before Evaluation: Evaluation with Unoptimised Prompts Can be Misleading
papers.summary: Current Large Language Model (LLM) evaluation frameworks utilize the same static prompt template across all models under evaluation. This differs from the common industry practice of using prompt optimization (PO) techniques to optimize the prompt for each model to maximize application performance. In this paper, we investigate the effect of PO towards LLM evaluations. Our results on public academic and internal industry benchmarks show that PO greatly affects the final ranking of models. This highlights the importance of practitioners performing PO per model when conducting evaluations to choose the best model for a given task.

ai.tldr: 当前LLM评估使用静态提示模板，而工业界常用提示优化（PO）提升性能。研究发现PO显著影响模型排名，建议评估时对每个模型进行PO。
ai.motivation: 现有评估框架对所有模型使用相同静态提示，与工业界为每个模型优化提示的做法脱节，可能导致评估结果失真。
ai.method: 在公开学术和内部工业基准上，对比使用与不使用提示优化时LLM的性能排名变化。
ai.result: 提示优化显著改变了模型的最终排名，说明未优化提示的评估可能产生误导。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解在评估LLM时，提示优化对排名结果的关键影响，避免因忽略优化而选错模型。

idea_spark.idea: 开发一个自动提示优化与评估集成框架，为每个候选模型动态生成最优提示并输出公平排名。
idea_spark.risk: 提示优化可能引入额外计算成本，且优化过程本身需标准化以避免过拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受工业界实践启发，将提示优化从应用环节前置到评估环节，提升评估的生态效度。

zh.tldr: 当前LLM评估使用静态提示模板，而工业界常用提示优化（PO）提升性能。研究发现PO显著影响模型排名，建议评估时对每个模型进行PO。
zh.motivation: 现有评估框架对所有模型使用相同静态提示，与工业界为每个模型优化提示的做法脱节，可能导致评估结果失真。
zh.method: 在公开学术和内部工业基准上，对比使用与不使用提示优化时LLM的性能排名变化。
zh.result: 提示优化显著改变了模型的最终排名，说明未优化提示的评估可能产生误导。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解在评估LLM时，提示优化对排名结果的关键影响，避免因忽略优化而选错模型。

en.tldr: Current LLM evaluations use static prompts, while industry often applies prompt optimization (PO). This study shows PO significantly alters model rankings, urging PO per model for fair evaluation.
en.motivation: Standard evaluation frameworks employ identical static prompts for all models, diverging from industry practice of optimizing prompts per model, which may bias rankings.
en.method: Compared model rankings on public academic and internal industry benchmarks with and without prompt optimization.
en.result: Prompt optimization substantially changed final model rankings, indicating that evaluations without optimization can be misleading.
en.help_to_user: Highlights the critical impact of prompt optimization on LLM evaluation rankings, helping practitioners avoid suboptimal model selection.

idea_spark.idea: Develop an integrated framework that automatically optimizes prompts for each candidate model and produces fair rankings.
idea_spark.risk: Prompt optimization may introduce additional computational cost and requires standardization to avoid overfitting.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by industry practice, this work shifts prompt optimization from application to evaluation stage to improve ecological validity.

papers.title: Residual Gaussian Splatting for Ultra Sparse-View CBCT Reconstruction
papers.summary: While 3D Gaussian splatting (3DGS) offers explicit and efficient scene representations for cone-beam computed tomography reconstruction, conventional photometric optimization inherently suffers from spectral bias under ultra sparse-view conditions, leading to over-smoothing and a loss of high-frequency anatomical details. Since wavelet transforms provide rich high-frequency information and have been widely utilized to enhance sparse reconstruction, this work integrates wavelet multi-resolution analysis with 3DGS. To circumvent the mathematical mismatch between the strict non-negativity of physical X-ray attenuation and the bipolar nature of high-frequency wavelet coefficients, we propose Residual Gaussian Splatting (RGS). Methodologically, we introduce a spectrally-decoupled Gaussian representation that stratifies the volumetric field into a geometric base component and a residual detail component. This decomposition systematically transforms explicit high-frequency fitting into a physically consistent, implicit residual compensation task. Furthermore, we devise a spectral-spatial collaborative optimization strategy to coordinate the interplay between geometric anchoring and texture refinement, effectively preventing spectral crosstalk. Extensive experiments on clinical datasets demonstrate that RGS enables the reconstructed images to capture highly refined geometric textures. It successfully resolves the trade-off between artifact suppression and detail preservation, yielding superior visual fidelity in complex trabecular and vascular structures compared to existing neural rendering baselines.

ai.tldr: 提出残差高斯泼溅（RGS），通过频谱解耦的高斯表示和频谱-空间协同优化，解决超稀疏视角CBCT重建中的频谱偏差问题，保留高频解剖细节。
ai.motivation: 传统3DGS在超稀疏视角下存在频谱偏差，导致过度平滑和丢失高频细节；而小波变换提供丰富高频信息但存在数学不匹配。
ai.method: 引入频谱解耦的高斯表示，将体积场分解为几何基分量和残差细节分量，将显式高频拟合转化为物理一致的隐式残差补偿任务，并设计频谱-空间协同优化策略。
ai.result: 在临床数据集上，RGS能捕获精细几何纹理，有效解决伪影抑制与细节保留之间的权衡，在复杂骨小梁和血管结构上优于现有神经渲染基线。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何结合小波多分辨率分析与3DGS，实现超稀疏视角CBCT的高质量重建。

idea_spark.idea: 将体积场分解为几何基和残差细节，利用小波高频信息进行隐式补偿，避免数学不匹配。
idea_spark.risk: 残差补偿可能引入额外计算开销，且对噪声敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从频谱偏差和小波变换的互补性出发，设计物理一致的隐式高频拟合方法。

en.tldr: Proposes Residual Gaussian Splatting (RGS) with spectrally-decoupled Gaussian representation and spectral-spatial collaborative optimization to address spectral bias in ultra sparse-view CBCT reconstruction, preserving high-frequency anatomical details.
en.motivation: Conventional 3DGS suffers from spectral bias under ultra sparse views, causing over-smoothing and loss of high-frequency details; wavelet transforms provide rich high-frequency information but face mathematical mismatch.
en.method: Introduces a spectrally-decoupled Gaussian representation that stratifies the volumetric field into a geometric base component and a residual detail component, transforming explicit high-frequency fitting into a physically consistent implicit residual compensation task, along with a spectral-spatial collaborative optimization strategy.
en.result: On clinical datasets, RGS captures refined geometric textures, successfully resolves the trade-off between artifact suppression and detail preservation, outperforming existing neural rendering baselines on complex trabecular and vascular structures.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to integrate wavelet multi-resolution analysis with 3DGS for high-quality ultra sparse-view CBCT reconstruction.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose the volumetric field into geometric base and residual detail, leveraging wavelet high-frequency information for implicit compensation to avoid mathematical mismatch.
idea_spark.risk: Residual compensation may introduce extra computational cost and be sensitive to noise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the complementarity of spectral bias and wavelet transforms to design a physically consistent implicit high-frequency fitting method.

papers.title: Fairness for distribution network operations and planning
papers.summary: The incorporation of fairness into the distribution network (DN) planning and operation has become a key goal of recent studies. The cost of implementing fairness, denominated the price of fairness (PoF), covers the efficiency that is renounced for attaining social cohesion through fair outcomes. Locational disparity makes fairness schemes emerge to level the consumers playing field. However, fairness encompasses a range of notions. From egalitarian to merit-based criteria, various metrics are implemented as a tool for measuring equitable utility distribution. These have different mathematical complexities, from linear to non-linear programming cases, which affect their overall applicability. Hence, this study compiles the overarching fairness notions and metrics, reviewing how these affect stakeholders and the inherent mathematical optimisation in resource allocation problems. The aim is to support consistent and transparent planning and decision-making within DN operations.

ai.tldr: 综述了配电网规划与运行中公平性的概念、度量及其对资源分配优化的影响，并提出了公平代价的概念。
ai.motivation: 配电网中位置差异导致用户间不公平，需要引入公平性机制来平衡各方利益。
ai.method: 系统梳理了从平均主义到绩效主义的多种公平性度量，并分析了其数学复杂度（线性/非线性规划）对适用性的影响。
ai.result: 明确了不同公平性定义对利益相关者和优化问题的影响，为配电网决策提供透明一致的框架。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解配电网公平性的多种定义及其数学实现，指导实际规划与运行中的公平性权衡。

idea_spark.idea: 结合多目标优化与公平性约束，设计自适应公平性调节算法，动态平衡效率与公平。
idea_spark.risk: 过度追求公平可能导致系统效率显著下降，且不同公平性定义间的冲突难以调和。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴社会福利函数思想，将公平性作为优化目标而非约束，探索帕累托最优边界。

en.tldr: A review of fairness concepts, metrics, and their impact on optimization in distribution network planning and operation, introducing the price of fairness.
en.motivation: Locational disparity in distribution networks creates inequity among consumers, necessitating fairness mechanisms.
en.method: Systematically categorizes fairness notions from egalitarian to merit-based, analyzing their mathematical complexity (linear/nonlinear programming) and applicability.
en.result: Clarifies how different fairness definitions affect stakeholders and optimization problems, providing a transparent framework for decision-making.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand various fairness definitions and their mathematical implementations for practical trade-offs in distribution network operations.

idea_spark.idea: Design an adaptive fairness algorithm that dynamically balances efficiency and equity using multi-objective optimization with fairness constraints.
idea_spark.risk: Overemphasis on fairness may significantly reduce system efficiency, and conflicts between different fairness definitions are hard to reconcile.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrow from social welfare functions to treat fairness as an optimization objective rather than a constraint, exploring Pareto frontiers.

papers.title: GourNet: A CNN-Based Model for Mango Leaf Disease Detection
papers.summary: Mango cultivation is crucial in the agricultural sector, significantly contributing to economic development and food security. However, diseases affecting mango leaves can significantly reduce both the production and overall fruit grade. Detecting leaf diseases at an early stage with precision is key to effective disease prevention and sustaining crop productivity. In this paper, we introduce a "deep learning" model named "GourNet", which leverages "Convolutional Neural Networks" to identify infections in mango leaves. We utilize the "MangoLeafBD" (MBD) dataset to train and assess the effectiveness of the presented model. The MBD dataset contains seven disease classes and a Healthy class, making a total of eight classes. To enhance model performance, the images are preprocessed through steps like resizing, rescaling, and data augmentation prior to training. To properly evaluate the model, the dataset is separated into 80% for training, with the remaining 20% equally split between validation and testing. Our model uses only 683,656 total parameters and achieves a classification accuracy of 97%. This research's source code can be found at: https://github.com/ekramalam/GourNet-Repo.

ai.tldr: 提出基于CNN的GourNet模型，在MangoLeafBD数据集上以97%准确率识别芒果叶片病害。
ai.motivation: 芒果叶片病害严重影响产量和果实等级，早期精准检测对病害防控和作物可持续生产至关重要。
ai.method: 使用MangoLeafBD数据集（8类，含健康类），经缩放、重缩放和数据增强预处理，以80%训练、10%验证、10%测试划分，构建仅683,656参数的CNN模型GourNet。
ai.result: 模型在测试集上达到97%的分类准确率。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助农业从业者或研究人员快速、准确地识别芒果叶片病害，支持早期干预。

idea_spark.idea: 结合迁移学习或注意力机制，进一步提升模型在复杂背景下的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 数据集规模有限且可能缺乏真实田间多样性，泛化能力需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用小参数模型实现高精度，启发在资源受限设备上部署农业AI应用。

en.tldr: Proposes GourNet, a CNN-based model that achieves 97% accuracy on the MangoLeafBD dataset for mango leaf disease detection.
en.motivation: Mango leaf diseases significantly reduce yield and fruit quality; early and precise detection is key to disease prevention and sustainable crop productivity.
en.method: Uses the MangoLeafBD dataset (8 classes including healthy), preprocessed via resizing, rescaling, and data augmentation, split into 80% training, 10% validation, 10% testing, and builds a CNN model GourNet with only 683,656 parameters.
en.result: The model achieves 97% classification accuracy on the test set.
en.help_to_user: Helps agricultural practitioners or researchers quickly and accurately detect mango leaf diseases, enabling early intervention.

idea_spark.idea: Combine transfer learning or attention mechanisms to further improve robustness in complex backgrounds.
idea_spark.risk: The dataset is limited in size and may lack real-field diversity, so generalization needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Achieving high accuracy with a small parameter count inspires deployment of agricultural AI on resource-constrained devices.

papers.title: Reliability-based Topology Optimization using Large Deviation Theory
papers.summary: Reliability-based topology optimization (RBTO) requires repeated estimation of small failure probabilities and their gradients, making conventional nested Monte Carlo approaches computationally prohibitive for large scale structural problems. We propose an RBTO framework that integrates large deviation theory~(LDT) with stochastic gradient descent~(SGD) to address this challenge. LDT provides closed-form exponential rate estimates of rare event probabilities, enabling accurate gradient computation without parametric assumptions on the failure density and without evaluating a full nested reliability loop at every iteration. These LDT-based gradient estimates are used directly to drive a mini batch SGD update of the design variables using only a few random samples per iteration. The framework is validated on three benchmarks, namely, a two-dimentional (2D) simply supported rectangular beam, a 2D L-shaped beam, and a three-dimentional (3D) cantilever beam, under both compliance-based and stress-based failure criteria. Across these examples, the RBTO designs achieve failure probabilities lower than their robust topology optimization counterparts, demonstrating that optimizing for performance under uncertainty alone does not guarantee the satisfaction of explicit reliability constraints. Therefore, the proposed framework offers a computationally efficient route to reliable structural design under uncertainty, with direct relevance to safety-critical engineering applications.

ai.tldr: 提出一种结合大偏差理论与随机梯度下降的可靠性拓扑优化框架，高效估计小失效概率及其梯度，避免嵌套蒙特卡洛循环。
ai.motivation: 传统可靠性拓扑优化需要反复估计小失效概率和梯度，嵌套蒙特卡洛方法计算成本过高，难以用于大规模结构问题。
ai.method: 利用大偏差理论提供失效概率的闭式指数率估计，无需对失效密度做参数假设，也无需每步执行完整嵌套可靠性循环；基于该梯度估计驱动小批量随机梯度下降更新设计变量。
ai.result: 在二维简支梁、L形梁和三维悬臂梁三个基准算例中，所得设计失效概率低于鲁棒拓扑优化结果，表明仅考虑不确定性下的性能优化不能保证满足显式可靠性约束。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种计算高效的可靠性拓扑优化方法，适用于安全关键工程结构设计。

idea_spark.idea: 将大偏差理论与随机梯度下降结合，避免嵌套蒙特卡洛，实现高效梯度估计。
idea_spark.risk: 大偏差理论在非指数型尾部分布或高维非线性问题中的近似精度可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用稀有事件概率的渐近理论替代昂贵模拟，为不确定性下优化提供新思路。

zh.help_to_user: 提供一种计算高效的可靠性拓扑优化方法，适用于安全关键工程结构设计。

idea_spark.risk: 大偏差理论在非指数型尾部分布或高维非线性问题中的近似精度可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用稀有事件概率的渐近理论替代昂贵模拟，为不确定性下优化提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes a reliability-based topology optimization framework integrating large deviation theory with stochastic gradient descent to efficiently estimate small failure probabilities and their gradients, avoiding nested Monte Carlo loops.
en.motivation: Conventional RBTO requires repeated estimation of small failure probabilities and gradients, making nested Monte Carlo computationally prohibitive for large-scale structural problems.
en.method: Uses large deviation theory to provide closed-form exponential rate estimates of failure probabilities without parametric assumptions on failure density or full nested reliability loops; these gradient estimates drive mini-batch SGD updates of design variables.
en.result: On three benchmarks (2D simply supported beam, 2D L-shaped beam, 3D cantilever beam), RBTO designs achieve lower failure probabilities than robust topology optimization counterparts, showing that optimizing for performance under uncertainty alone does not guarantee explicit reliability constraints.
en.help_to_user: Offers a computationally efficient route to reliable structural design under uncertainty, relevant to safety-critical engineering applications.

idea_spark.idea: Combines large deviation theory with stochastic gradient descent to avoid nested Monte Carlo and achieve efficient gradient estimation.
idea_spark.risk: Approximation accuracy of large deviation theory may degrade for non-exponential tail distributions or high-dimensional nonlinear problems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging asymptotic theory of rare event probabilities to replace expensive simulations opens new avenues for optimization under uncertainty.

papers.title: Representative Spectral Correlation Network for Multi-source Remote Sensing Image Classification
papers.summary: Hyperspectral image (HSI) and SAR/LiDAR data offer complementary spectral and structural information for land-cover classification. However, their effective fusion remains challenging due to two major limitations: The spectral redundancy in high-dimensional HSI and the heterogeneous characteristics between multi-source data. To this end, we propose Representative Spectral Correlation Network (RSCNet), a novel multi-source image classification framework specifically designed to address the above challenges through spectral selection and adaptive interaction. The network incorporates two key components: (1) Key Band Selection Module (KBSM) that adaptively selects task-relevant spectral bands from the original HSI under cross-source guidance, thereby alleviating redundancy and mitigating information loss from conventional PCA-based spectral reduction. Moreover, the learned band subset exhibits highly discriminative spectral structures that align with discriminative semantic cues, promoting compact yet expressive representations. (2) Cross-source Adaptive Fusion Module (CAFM) that performs cross-source attention weighting and local-global contextual refinement to enhance cross-source feature interaction. Experiments on three public benchmark datasets demonstrate that our RSCNet achieves superior performance compared with state-of-the-art methods, while maintaining substantially lower computational complexity. Our codes are publicly available at https://github.com/oucailab/RSCNet.

ai.tldr: 提出代表性光谱相关网络（RSCNet），通过关键波段选择和跨源自适应融合，有效融合高光谱与SAR/LiDAR数据，实现高精度地物分类。
ai.motivation: 高光谱图像与SAR/LiDAR数据融合面临光谱冗余和异质性两大挑战，现有方法（如PCA降维）易丢失信息。
ai.method: RSCNet包含关键波段选择模块（KBSM）和跨源自适应融合模块（CAFM），前者在跨源引导下自适应选取任务相关波段，后者通过注意力加权和局部-全局上下文精炼增强跨源交互。
ai.result: 在三个公开基准数据集上，RSCNet以更低计算复杂度取得优于现有方法的分类性能。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种高效的多源遥感图像分类框架，代码已开源，可直接用于地物分类任务。

idea_spark.risk: 波段选择可能过度依赖训练数据分布，泛化到新场景时需重新调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受跨源引导的波段选择启发，可探索在无监督或半监督场景下自动发现模态间互补特征。

en.tldr: Proposes Representative Spectral Correlation Network (RSCNet) for multi-source remote sensing image classification, using key band selection and cross-source adaptive fusion to effectively combine hyperspectral and SAR/LiDAR data.
en.motivation: Fusion of hyperspectral and SAR/LiDAR data faces spectral redundancy and heterogeneity; conventional PCA-based reduction causes information loss.
en.method: RSCNet includes a Key Band Selection Module (KBSM) that adaptively selects task-relevant bands under cross-source guidance, and a Cross-source Adaptive Fusion Module (CAFM) that enhances interaction via attention weighting and local-global refinement.
en.result: On three public benchmarks, RSCNet achieves superior classification performance with lower computational complexity compared to state-of-the-art methods.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient multi-source remote sensing classification framework with open-source code, directly applicable to land-cover tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Combining key band selection with cross-source attention fusion reduces redundancy while preserving discriminative spectral structures.
idea_spark.risk: Band selection may overfit to training data distribution, requiring retuning for new scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by cross-source guided band selection, one could explore unsupervised or semi-supervised discovery of complementary features across modalities.

papers.title: Think it, Run it: Autonomous ML pipeline generation via self-healing multi-agent AI
papers.summary: The purpose of our paper is to develop a unified multi-agent architecture that automates end-to-end machine learning (ML) pipeline generation from datasets and natural-language (NL) goals, improving efficiency, robustness and explainability. A five-agent system is proposed to handle profiling, intent parsing, microservice recommendation, Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) construction and execution. It integrates code-grounded Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for microservice understanding, an explainable hybrid recommender combining multiple criteria, a self-healing mechanism using Large Language Model (LLM)-based error interpretation and adaptive learning from execution history. The approach is evaluated on 150 ML tasks across diverse scenarios. The system achieves an 84.7% end-to-end pipeline success rate, outperforming baseline methods. It demonstrates improved robustness through self-healing and reduces workflow development time compared to manual construction. The study introduces a novel integration of code-grounded RAG, explainable recommendation, self-healing execution and adaptive learning within a single architecture, showing that tightly coupled intelligent components can outperform isolated solutions.

ai.tldr: 提出一个五智能体架构，通过自愈机制和代码增强检索生成，从数据集和自然语言目标自动生成端到端机器学习流水线，成功率84.7%。
ai.motivation: 手动构建ML流水线耗时且易错，现有自动化方法缺乏鲁棒性和可解释性。
ai.method: 五智能体系统：分析数据、解析意图、推荐微服务、构建DAG、执行并自愈。集成代码增强RAG、可解释混合推荐、LLM错误解释和自适应学习。
ai.result: 在150个任务上端到端成功率84.7%，优于基线，减少开发时间，提升鲁棒性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户从自然语言描述快速生成可靠的ML流水线，减少手动调试。

idea_spark.idea: 将代码增强RAG与可解释推荐结合，使智能体不仅推荐服务还能解释原因。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖LLM的错误解释可能引入新错误，需要高质量执行历史。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受软件工程中自愈系统启发，将类似概念引入ML流水线自动化。

idea_spark.inspiration: 受软件工程中自愈系统启发，将类似概念引入ML流水线自动化。

en.tldr: Proposes a five-agent architecture that autonomously generates end-to-end ML pipelines from datasets and natural-language goals, achieving 84.7% success rate via self-healing and code-grounded RAG.
en.motivation: Manual ML pipeline construction is time-consuming and error-prone; existing automation lacks robustness and explainability.
en.method: Five-agent system: profiling, intent parsing, microservice recommendation, DAG construction, and execution with self-healing. Integrates code-grounded RAG, explainable hybrid recommender, LLM-based error interpretation, and adaptive learning.
en.result: 84.7% end-to-end success rate on 150 tasks, outperforming baselines, reducing development time, and improving robustness.
en.help_to_user: Helps users quickly generate reliable ML pipelines from natural-language goals, reducing manual debugging.

idea_spark.idea: Combine code-grounded RAG with explainable recommendation so agents not only recommend services but also explain why.
idea_spark.risk: LLM-based error interpretation may introduce new errors; requires high-quality execution history.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by self-healing systems in software engineering, applying similar concepts to ML pipeline automation.

papers.title: On the Lifespan of Axisymmetric Hall-MHD with Swirl
papers.summary: In this paper, we study the three-dimensional inviscid incompressible resistive Hall-MHD system in the axisymmetric setting with nontrivial swirl velocity and purely azimuthal magnetic. Assuming only that the swirl component of the initial velocity is sufficiently small, we prove that the lifespan of the strong solution can be sufficiently large. An explicit lifespan lower bound in terms of the size of the initial swirl is given. Moreover, we also study the behavior of the lifespan as the resistivity tends to zero.

ai.tldr: 研究轴对称带旋流的无粘不可压缩电阻Hall-MHD系统，证明初始旋流足够小时强解寿命可任意长，并给出寿命下界及电阻趋于零时的行为。
ai.motivation: 理解旋流和电阻对Hall-MHD系统解寿命的影响，填补轴对称带旋流情形下理论分析的空白。
ai.method: 利用能量估计和精细的偏微分方程分析，建立旋流大小与寿命下界之间的显式关系。
ai.result: 初始旋流足够小时，强解寿命可以足够大；电阻趋于零时寿命行为得到刻画。
ai.help_to_user: 提供轴对称Hall-MHD系统解长期存在的充分条件，为数值模拟和物理应用提供理论指导。

idea_spark.idea: 探索旋流与磁场相互作用如何影响解的爆破时间，或可设计控制旋流来延长解寿命。
idea_spark.risk: 理论结果依赖轴对称和纯方位角磁场假设，推广至一般三维情形可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 电阻作为小参数时的极限行为启发我们研究耗散机制对解长期行为的影响。

zh.tldr: 研究轴对称带旋流的无粘不可压缩电阻Hall-MHD系统，证明初始旋流足够小时强解寿命可任意长，并给出寿命下界及电阻趋于零时的行为。
zh.motivation: 理解旋流和电阻对Hall-MHD系统解寿命的影响，填补轴对称带旋流情形下理论分析的空白。

zh.help_to_user: 提供轴对称Hall-MHD系统解长期存在的充分条件，为数值模拟和物理应用提供理论指导。

idea_spark.idea: 探索旋流与磁场相互作用如何影响解的爆破时间，或可设计控制旋流来延长解寿命。
idea_spark.risk: 理论结果依赖轴对称和纯方位角磁场假设，推广至一般三维情形可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 电阻作为小参数时的极限行为启发我们研究耗散机制对解长期行为的影响。

en.tldr: We study the 3D inviscid incompressible resistive Hall-MHD system with axisymmetry, nontrivial swirl velocity, and purely azimuthal magnetic field. We prove that if the initial swirl is sufficiently small, the strong solution lifespan can be arbitrarily large, and we give an explicit lower bound. The behavior as resistivity tends to zero is also analyzed.
en.motivation: To understand how swirl and resistivity affect the lifespan of solutions in Hall-MHD, filling a theoretical gap for axisymmetric flows with swirl.
en.method: Energy estimates and refined PDE analysis to establish an explicit relationship between swirl magnitude and lifespan lower bound.
en.result: Sufficiently small initial swirl yields arbitrarily large lifespan; the vanishing resistivity limit is characterized.
en.help_to_user: Provides sufficient conditions for long-time existence of axisymmetric Hall-MHD solutions, guiding numerical simulations and physical applications.

idea_spark.idea: Explore how swirl-magnetic interaction affects blow-up time; possibly design swirl control to prolong solution existence.
idea_spark.risk: Results rely on axisymmetry and purely azimuthal magnetic field; generalization to full 3D may fail.
idea_spark.inspiration: The vanishing resistivity limit inspires study of how dissipation mechanisms influence long-time behavior.

papers.title: Position-Aware Drafting for Inference Acceleration in LLM-Based Generative List-Wise Recommendation
papers.summary: Large language model (LLM)-based generative list-wise recommendation has advanced rapidly, but decoding remains sequential and thus latency-prone. To accelerate inference without changing the target distribution, speculative decoding (SD) uses a small draft model to propose several next tokens at once and a target LLM to verify and accept the longest prefix, skipping multiple steps per round. In generative recommendation, however, each item is represented by multiple semantic-ID tokens, often with separators, and current drafts typically treat these tokens uniformly. This overlooks two practical facts: (i) a token's semantics depend on its within-item slot, and (ii) uncertainty tends to increase with speculation depth. Without modeling these effects, SD's speedups can be limited. We introduce PAD-Rec, Position-Aware Drafting for generative Recommendation, a lightweight module that augments the draft model with two complementary signals. Item position embeddings explicitly encode the within-item slot of each token, strengthening structural awareness. Step position embeddings encode the draft step, allowing the model to adapt to depth-dependent uncertainty and improve proposal quality. To harmonize these signals with base features, we add simple gates: a learnable coefficient for item slots and a context-driven gate for draft steps. The module is trainable, easy to integrate with standard draft models, and adds negligible inference overhead. Extensive experiments on four real-world datasets show up to 3.1x wall-clock speedup and about 5% average wall-clock speedup gain over strong SD baselines, while largely preserving recommendation quality.

ai.tldr: 提出位置感知草稿模块PAD-Rec，通过物品位置嵌入和步骤位置嵌入增强草稿模型的结构感知与不确定性适应能力，在生成式列表推荐中实现最高3.1倍加速。
ai.motivation: 基于LLM的生成式列表推荐中，推测解码的草稿模型未考虑令牌在物品内的位置语义以及推测深度带来的不确定性，导致加速效果受限。
ai.method: 设计轻量级PAD-Rec模块，为草稿模型添加物品位置嵌入（编码令牌在物品内的槽位）和步骤位置嵌入（编码推测步骤），并通过可学习系数和上下文门控融合基础特征。
ai.result: 在四个真实数据集上，相比强基线获得约5%的平均加速增益，最高达3.1倍墙钟加速，同时基本保持推荐质量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不改变目标分布的前提下，通过位置感知草稿显著提升生成式推荐系统的推理速度。

idea_spark.idea: 将物品位置嵌入与步骤位置嵌入结合门控机制，使草稿模型能动态调整对不同深度推测的置信度。
idea_spark.risk: 门控机制可能增加少量训练开销，且对长序列或高不确定性场景的泛化性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从推荐系统中物品结构化的特点出发，将位置信息显式建模到推测解码中，启发其他结构化生成任务（如代码生成、表格生成）的加速方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将物品位置嵌入与步骤位置嵌入结合门控机制，使草稿模型能动态调整对不同深度推测的置信度。
idea_spark.risk: 门控机制可能增加少量训练开销，且对长序列或高不确定性场景的泛化性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从推荐系统中物品结构化的特点出发，将位置信息显式建模到推测解码中，启发其他结构化生成任务（如代码生成、表格生成）的加速方法。

en.tldr: Proposes PAD-Rec, a position-aware drafting module that augments draft models with item position embeddings and step position embeddings, achieving up to 3.1x speedup in generative list-wise recommendation.
en.motivation: In LLM-based generative list-wise recommendation, speculative decoding draft models treat all tokens uniformly, ignoring within-item slot semantics and depth-dependent uncertainty, limiting speedups.
en.method: Designs a lightweight PAD-Rec module that adds item position embeddings (encoding token slot within an item) and step position embeddings (encoding draft step) to the draft model, fused via learnable coefficients and context-driven gates.
en.result: On four real-world datasets, achieves up to 3.1x wall-clock speedup and about 5% average speedup gain over strong SD baselines while largely preserving recommendation quality.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how position-aware drafting can significantly accelerate inference in generative recommendation systems without altering the target distribution.

idea_spark.idea: Combine item position embeddings and step position embeddings with gating mechanisms to enable the draft model to dynamically adjust confidence across speculation depths.
idea_spark.risk: Gating mechanisms may introduce slight training overhead, and generalization to long sequences or high-uncertainty scenarios requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Explicitly modeling positional structure in speculative decoding, inspired by item structure in recommendation, suggests acceleration methods for other structured generation tasks (e.g., code generation, table generation).

papers.title: AttriBE: Quantifying Attribute Expressivity in Body Embeddings for Recognition and Identification
papers.summary: Person re-identification (ReID) systems that match individuals across images or video frames are essential in many real-world applications. However, existing methods are often influenced by attributes such as gender, pose, and body mass index (BMI), which vary in unconstrained settings and raise concerns related to fairness and generalization. To address this, we extend the notion of expressivity, defined as the mutual information between learned features and specific attributes, using a secondary neural network to quantify how strongly attributes are encoded. Applying this framework to three transformer-based ReID models on a large-scale visible-spectrum dataset, we find that BMI consistently shows the highest expressivity in deeper layers. Attributes in the final representation are ranked as BMI > Pitch > Gender > Yaw, and expressivity evolves across layers and training epochs, with pose peaking in intermediate layers and BMI strengthening with depth. We further extend the analysis to cross-spectral person identification across infrared modalities including short-wave, medium-wave, and long-wave infrared. In this setting, pitch becomes comparable to BMI and attribute trends increase monotonically across depth, suggesting increased reliance on structural cues when bridging modality gaps. Overall, the results show that transformer-based ReID embeddings encode a hierarchy of implicit attributes, with morphometric information persistently embedded and pose contributing more strongly under cross-spectral conditions.

ai.tldr: 提出AttriBE框架，用互信息量化行人重识别模型中身体嵌入的属性表达性，发现BMI在深层表达最强，跨光谱时姿态贡献增大。
ai.motivation: 现有ReID模型受性别、姿态、BMI等属性影响，在无约束场景下公平性和泛化性不足，需要量化属性编码强度。
ai.method: 扩展表达性概念，用辅助神经网络计算学习特征与属性间的互信息，在三个Transformer模型上分析属性表达性随层和训练轮次的变化。
ai.result: 最终表示中属性排序为BMI>俯仰角>性别>偏航角；跨光谱识别中俯仰角与BMI相当，属性趋势随深度单调递增。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解ReID模型隐式编码的属性层次，指导公平性改进和跨模态泛化。

idea_spark.idea: 利用属性表达性分析设计去偏训练策略，或构建属性感知的模型压缩方法。
idea_spark.risk: 互信息估计可能受神经网络容量影响，导致属性表达性度量偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 跨光谱中姿态贡献增强启发利用结构线索设计鲁棒的跨模态匹配方法。

zh.tldr: 提出AttriBE框架，用互信息量化行人重识别模型中身体嵌入的属性表达性，发现BMI在深层表达最强，跨光谱时姿态贡献增大。
zh.motivation: 现有ReID模型受性别、姿态、BMI等属性影响，在无约束场景下公平性和泛化性不足，需要量化属性编码强度。
zh.method: 扩展表达性概念，用辅助神经网络计算学习特征与属性间的互信息，在三个Transformer模型上分析属性表达性随层和训练轮次的变化。
zh.result: 最终表示中属性排序为BMI>俯仰角>性别>偏航角；跨光谱识别中俯仰角与BMI相当，属性趋势随深度单调递增。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解ReID模型隐式编码的属性层次，指导公平性改进和跨模态泛化。

idea_spark.idea: 利用属性表达性分析设计去偏训练策略，或构建属性感知的模型压缩方法。
idea_spark.risk: 互信息估计可能受神经网络容量影响，导致属性表达性度量偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 跨光谱中姿态贡献增强启发利用结构线索设计鲁棒的跨模态匹配方法。

en.tldr: Proposes AttriBE to quantify attribute expressivity in person re-identification body embeddings via mutual information, finding BMI most expressive in deeper layers and pose more dominant under cross-spectral conditions.
en.motivation: Existing ReID models are influenced by attributes like gender, pose, and BMI, raising fairness and generalization concerns; need to quantify how strongly attributes are encoded.
en.method: Extends the notion of expressivity as mutual information between learned features and attributes using a secondary neural network; analyzes three transformer-based models on visible and cross-spectral datasets.
en.result: Attribute ranking in final representation: BMI > Pitch > Gender > Yaw; cross-spectral identification shows pitch comparable to BMI and monotonic increase of attribute trends with depth.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand implicit attribute hierarchies in ReID embeddings, guiding fairness improvements and cross-modal generalization.

idea_spark.idea: Use attribute expressivity analysis to design debiasing training strategies or build attribute-aware model compression methods.
idea_spark.risk: Mutual information estimation may be biased by neural network capacity, leading to inaccurate expressivity measures.
idea_spark.inspiration: The increased contribution of pose in cross-spectral settings inspires designing robust cross-modal matching methods leveraging structural cues.

papers.title: Training-Free Tunnel Defect Inspection and Engineering Interpretation via Visual Recalibration and Entity Reconstruction
papers.summary: Tunnel inspection requires outputs that can support defect localization, measurement, severity grading, and engineering documentation. Existing training-free foundation-model pipelines usually stop at coarse open-vocabulary proposals, which are difficult to use directly in interference-heavy tunnel scenes. We propose a training-free framework TunnelMIND. Specifically, language-guided defect proposals are not treated as final outputs; instead, their spatial support is recalibrated at inference time through dense visual consistency, so that coarse semantic anchors can be transformed into more reliable prompts under tunnel-specific hard negatives. The resulting masks are further reconstructed into structured defect entities with category, location, geometry, severity, and context attributes, which are then mapped to retrieval-grounded explanation and engineering-readable report generation under expert knowledge constraints. On visible, GPR, and road defect tasks, TunnelMIND achieves F1 scores of 0.68, 0.78, and 0.72, respectively. Overall, TunnelMIND shows that training-free tunnel inspection can move beyond coarse localization toward structured defect evidence for engineering assessment.

ai.tldr: 提出无需训练的隧道缺陷检测框架TunnelMIND，通过视觉重校准和实体重建，将粗粒度语义锚点转化为结构化缺陷证据，支持工程评估。
ai.motivation: 现有无训练基础模型在隧道场景中仅输出粗粒度开放词汇提议，难以直接用于干扰严重的隧道缺陷定位、测量和分级。
ai.method: 利用语言引导的缺陷提议，通过密集视觉一致性在推理时重校准空间支持，再将掩码重建为包含类别、位置、几何、严重度和上下文属性的结构化缺陷实体，并映射到基于检索的解释和工程可读报告。
ai.result: 在可见光、探地雷达和道路缺陷任务上分别达到0.68、0.78和0.72的F1分数。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户从隧道检测图像中自动获取结构化缺陷证据，包括位置、尺寸、严重等级和工程解释，无需额外训练。

papers.title: BV quantization of $φ^3$-theory on $λ$-Minkowski space: Tree-level correlation functions
papers.summary: We review the quantization of scalar field theory on $λ$-Minkowski space using the Batalin--Vilkovisky (BV) formalism. We consider $φ^3$-theory in two different quantization schemes: standard and braided. While standard BV quantization is based on an ordinary $L_\infty$-algebra, braided BV quantization is based on a braided $L_\infty$-algebra. We compare the tree-level three-point and four-point correlation functions in the two approaches. For the four-point function, standard quantization leads to two inequivalent classes of diagrams with different noncommutative contributions, whereas braided quantization yields only a single class of diagrams with noncommutativity entering solely through an overall phase factor depending on the external momenta.

ai.tldr: 本文回顾了在λ-闵可夫斯基空间上使用Batalin-Vilkovisky形式论对标量场论进行量子化的方法，比较了标准量子化和编织量子化两种方案下的树图三点和四点关联函数。
ai.motivation: 研究非交换时空上的量子场论，特别是λ-闵可夫斯基空间上的φ^3理论，并比较不同量子化方案（标准与编织）对关联函数的影响。
ai.method: 使用Batalin-Vilkovisky形式论，分别基于普通L∞-代数和编织L∞-代数进行量子化，计算并比较树图三点和四点关联函数。
ai.result: 对于四点函数，标准量子化产生两类不等价图，具有不同的非交换贡献；而编织量子化只产生一类图，非交换性仅通过依赖于外动量的整体相位因子进入。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解非交换时空上量子场论的不同量子化方案及其对关联函数的影响，特别是编织量子化如何简化非交换贡献。

idea_spark.idea: 探索编织L∞-代数在更复杂场论（如规范理论或引力理论）中的应用。
idea_spark.risk: 编织量子化的数学结构可能更复杂，且其物理解释（如整体相位因子）需要进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 编织量子化中非交换性仅通过整体相位因子出现，这启发我们寻找类似简化非交换效应的其他对称性或代数结构。
