en.tldr: Proposes a dual-stream memory architecture that separates patient narrative from EHR, using a reconciliation engine to detect clinical discrepancies, achieving 84.4% detection rate across 675 health coaching sessions.
en.motivation: Longitudinal health agents rely on patient self-report and EHR, both imperfect; existing memory systems overwrite old facts with new statements, risking safety failures.
en.method: Designs a dual-stream memory architecture strictly separating patient narrative from structured clinical records (FHIR), governed by a reconciliation engine that classifies discrepancies by type, severity, and FHIR resources.
en.result: On 26 patients across 675 sessions, the engine detects 84.4% of designed discrepancies with 86.7% safety-critical recall, and quantifies a 13.6% error cascade.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to build safe longitudinal health agents by avoiding memory overwrite errors.

idea_spark.idea: Explore extending the reconciliation engine to proactively generate clarification questions when discrepancies are detected, reducing reliance on passive memory.
idea_spark.risk: The engine may over-rely on FHIR data, missing novel information from patient self-report, leading to misjudgment or delayed updates.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by clinical double-checking processes, applying similar principles to AI memory management.

papers.title: A MEC-Based Optimization Framework for Dynamic Inductive Charging
papers.summary: Range anxiety and long recharging times remain critical barriers to electric vehicle adoption. Dynamic Inductive Charging (DIC) offers a compelling solution by enabling wireless power transfer while driving, potentially reducing battery size requirements and thus vehicle costs. However, DIC infrastructures are expensive and power-constrained, requiring intelligent resource allocation to maximize user satisfaction and economic viability. We propose a Model Predictive Control framework for optimal power allocation in DIC systems, using edge computing and vehicular communications to prioritize vehicles with critical battery states. The framework is implemented and evaluated through SUMO-based simulations on a realistic 10 km urban scenario in Istanbul, Turkey, under varying traffic intensities. Results demonstrate two critical limitations of uncoordinated allocation. First, resource utilization remains suboptimal despite available power when demand saturates system capacity. Second, when demand exceeds capacity, uniform distribution of power leaves a heavy tail of critically unsatisfied vehicles that may require emergency stops. Our MPC-based strategy addresses both regimes -- maximizing power utilization during saturation through dynamic stripe rebalancing, and improving satisfaction fairness under scarcity by aggressively prioritizing depleted batteries at the expense of well-charged vehicles. The framework and simulation tools are released as open-source to support further research in this emerging domain.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于模型预测控制的动态感应充电优化框架，利用边缘计算和车联网通信，在伊斯坦布尔10公里城市场景的SUMO仿真中验证了其能提升资源利用率和公平性。
ai.motivation: 电动汽车的续航焦虑和充电时间长是主要障碍，动态感应充电（DIC）可在行驶中无线充电，但基础设施昂贵且功率受限，需要智能资源分配。
ai.method: 采用模型预测控制（MPC）框架，结合边缘计算和车辆通信，优先为电池临界状态的车辆分配功率，并通过动态条带再平衡和激进优先级策略应对不同负载情况。
ai.result: 在SUMO仿真中，MPC策略在饱和时最大化功率利用，在过载时改善公平性，减少严重不满意的车辆数量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解动态感应充电系统中的资源分配优化方法及其在真实城市场景下的效果。

idea_spark.idea: 结合强化学习进一步优化MPC中的优先级权重，或引入多目标优化平衡效率与公平。
idea_spark.risk: 实际部署中通信延迟和计算开销可能影响实时性，且仿真假设与真实交通流存在差距。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将车辆电池状态作为动态优先级指标，类似思路可用于其他移动设备的无线充电网络。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于模型预测控制的动态感应充电优化框架，利用边缘计算和车联网通信，在伊斯坦布尔10公里城市场景的SUMO仿真中验证了其能提升资源利用率和公平性。
zh.motivation: 电动汽车的续航焦虑和充电时间长是主要障碍，动态感应充电（DIC）可在行驶中无线充电，但基础设施昂贵且功率受限，需要智能资源分配。
zh.method: 采用模型预测控制（MPC）框架，结合边缘计算和车辆通信，优先为电池临界状态的车辆分配功率，并通过动态条带再平衡和激进优先级策略应对不同负载情况。
zh.result: 在SUMO仿真中，MPC策略在饱和时最大化功率利用，在过载时改善公平性，减少严重不满意的车辆数量。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解动态感应充电系统中的资源分配优化方法及其在真实城市场景下的效果。

idea_spark.idea: 结合强化学习进一步优化MPC中的优先级权重，或引入多目标优化平衡效率与公平。
idea_spark.risk: 实际部署中通信延迟和计算开销可能影响实时性，且仿真假设与真实交通流存在差距。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将车辆电池状态作为动态优先级指标，类似思路可用于其他移动设备的无线充电网络。

en.tldr: Proposes a Model Predictive Control framework for dynamic inductive charging, leveraging edge computing and vehicular communications to optimize power allocation, validated via SUMO simulations in a realistic 10 km urban scenario in Istanbul.
en.motivation: Range anxiety and long charging times hinder EV adoption; dynamic inductive charging (DIC) enables wireless power transfer while driving but requires intelligent resource allocation due to expensive and power-constrained infrastructure.
en.method: Uses a Model Predictive Control (MPC) framework with edge computing and vehicular communications to prioritize vehicles with critical battery states, employing dynamic stripe rebalancing and aggressive prioritization under different demand regimes.
en.result: SUMO simulations show the MPC strategy maximizes power utilization under saturation and improves fairness under scarcity, reducing the number of critically unsatisfied vehicles.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand resource allocation optimization in dynamic inductive charging systems and its performance in a realistic urban scenario.

idea_spark.idea: Combine reinforcement learning to further optimize priority weights in MPC, or introduce multi-objective optimization to balance efficiency and fairness.
idea_spark.risk: Communication latency and computational overhead may affect real-time performance in deployment, and simulation assumptions may differ from real traffic conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using vehicle battery state as a dynamic priority metric can inspire similar approaches for wireless charging networks of other mobile devices.

papers.title: Formulating Subgroup Discovery as a Quantum Optimization Problem for Network Security
papers.summary: While current network intrusion detection systems achieve satisfactory accuracy, they often lack explainability. Subgroup Discovery (SD) addresses this by building interpretable rules that characterize feature interactions associated with attack traffic. With large datasets, classical heuristic beam search methods struggle with exponentially scaling search spaces and can prune critical multi-feature interactions. This paper introduces a quantum-enhanced pipeline for SD applied to network intrusion detection using NSL-KDD, formulating SD as quantum optimization for the first time. By encoding feature selection as a Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO) and solving it via the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) on IBM Quantum hardware (ibm_pittsburgh), the pipeline identifies subgroups of network features that discriminate normal from attack traffic. A least-squares regression QUBO formulation fits the Weighted Relative Accuracy (WRAcc) landscape over feature subsets, with surrogate sampling for larger QUBOs. Results are benchmarked against exhaustive enumeration and Beam Search using ratios for Hamiltonian quality and WRAcc. Hardware scaling experiments on ibm_pittsburgh (10-30 qubits) reveal that QAOA at depth p = 1 shows WRAcc ratios of 0.983 at 10 qubits, 0.971 at 15 qubits, 0.855 at 20 qubits, and 0.624 at 25 qubits, degrading to 0.039 at 30 qubits as circuit noise dominates, establishing an empirical NISQ scaling boundary. Results demonstrate that QAOA discovers subgroups competitive with classical heuristics and finds multi-feature interaction patterns that greedy Beam Search prunes, with QAOA-unique subgroups achieving up to 99.6% test precision. This work establishes a framework for quantum combinatorial optimization in cybersecurity and characterizes hardware scaling for NISQ devices.

ai.tldr: 首次将子群发现问题形式化为量子优化问题，用于网络入侵检测，在IBM量子硬件上使用QAOA算法，在10-25量子比特范围内取得了与经典方法竞争的结果，并发现了经典贪婪搜索遗漏的多特征交互模式。
ai.motivation: 现有网络入侵检测系统准确率高但缺乏可解释性，子群发现能构建可解释规则，但经典启发式搜索在大数据集上因搜索空间指数增长而可能剪枝掉关键的多特征交互。
ai.method: 将特征选择编码为二次无约束二元优化（QUBO）问题，使用量子近似优化算法（QAOA）在IBM量子硬件上求解，通过最小二乘回归拟合WRAcc景观，并采用代理采样处理更大规模的QUBO。
ai.result: 在10-25量子比特上，QAOA的WRAcc比率从0.983下降到0.624，30量子比特时因电路噪声降至0.039；QAOA发现的子群在测试集上达到99.6%的精确度，且能发现贪婪波束搜索遗漏的多特征交互模式。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助网络安全研究人员理解如何利用量子计算提升子群发现的搜索能力，发现经典方法遗漏的关键特征交互，从而构建更准确且可解释的入侵检测规则。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将子群发现的QUBO公式与变分量子本征求解器（VQE）结合，或使用量子退火硬件（如D-Wave）处理更大规模的特征子集。
idea_spark.risk: 当前NISQ设备噪声限制了可用的量子比特数（约25量子比特），且QAOA深度p=1时性能已随规模下降，实际部署需考虑量子纠错或混合经典-量子方案。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受启发于量子优化在组合搜索中的潜力，可进一步研究如何将子群发现中的WRAcc目标函数直接编码为更高效的量子哈密顿量，或利用量子核方法增强特征交互的表示。

en.tldr: This paper formulates subgroup discovery as a quantum optimization problem for network intrusion detection, using QAOA on IBM quantum hardware to identify feature subsets that discriminate normal from attack traffic, achieving competitive results on 10-25 qubits and discovering multi-feature interactions missed by classical beam search.
en.motivation: Current network intrusion detection systems lack explainability; subgroup discovery can build interpretable rules, but classical heuristic search struggles with exponentially scaling search spaces and may prune critical multi-feature interactions on large datasets.
en.method: Feature selection is encoded as a Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO) problem, solved via the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) on IBM quantum hardware, with least-squares regression to fit the WRAcc landscape and surrogate sampling for larger QUBOs.
en.result: QAOA achieves WRAcc ratios from 0.983 at 10 qubits to 0.624 at 25 qubits, degrading to 0.039 at 30 qubits due to noise; QAOA-discovered subgroups reach 99.6% test precision and uncover multi-feature interactions pruned by greedy beam search.
en.help_to_user: Helps cybersecurity researchers leverage quantum computing to enhance subgroup discovery, uncovering critical feature interactions missed by classical methods for more accurate and interpretable intrusion detection rules.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining the QUBO formulation with variational quantum eigensolvers (VQE) or using quantum annealing hardware (e.g., D-Wave) for larger feature subsets.
idea_spark.risk: Current NISQ device noise limits usable qubits to around 25, and QAOA depth p=1 already shows performance degradation with scale; practical deployment may require quantum error correction or hybrid classical-quantum schemes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the potential of quantum optimization in combinatorial search, further research could encode the WRAcc objective directly into a more efficient quantum Hamiltonian or use quantum kernel methods to enhance feature interaction representation.

papers.title: Learning from Disagreement: Clinician Overrides as Implicit Preference Signals for Clinical AI in Value-Based Care
papers.summary: We reframe clinician overrides of clinical AI recommendations as implicit preference data - the same signal structure exploited by reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), but richer: the annotator is a domain expert, the alternatives carry real consequences, and downstream outcomes are observable. We present a formal framework extending standard preference learning with three contributions: a five-category override taxonomy mapping override types to distinct model update targets; a preference formulation conditioned on patient state s, organizational context c, and clinician capability kappa, where kappa decomposes into execution capability kappa-exec and alignment capability kappa-align; and a dual learning architecture that jointly trains a reward model and a capability model via alternating optimization, preventing a failure mode we term suppression bias-the systematic suppression of correct-but-difficult recommendations when clinician capability falls below the execution threshold. We argue that chronic disease management under outcome-based payment contracts produces override data with uniquely favorable properties-longitudinal density, concentrated decision space, outcome labels, and natural capability variation-and that training environments combining longitudinal outcome measurement with aligned financial incentives are a necessary condition for learning a reward model aligned with patient trajectory rather than with encounter economics. This framework emerged from operational work to improve clinician capability in a live value-based care deployment.

ai.tldr: 将临床医生对AI建议的覆盖操作重新定义为隐式偏好数据，并提出了一个扩展的偏好学习框架，包含覆盖分类法、条件偏好公式和双学习架构，以解决抑制偏差问题。
ai.motivation: 临床医生覆盖AI建议的行为包含丰富的领域知识和结果信息，但现有方法未充分利用这些数据来改进AI模型。
ai.method: 提出了五类覆盖分类法、基于患者状态、组织环境和临床医生能力的条件偏好公式，以及联合训练奖励模型和能力模型的双学习架构。
ai.result: 该框架能防止抑制偏差，即当临床医生能力低于执行阈值时系统性地压制正确但困难的建议。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员和从业者利用临床覆盖数据训练更符合患者长期轨迹的AI模型，特别适用于基于价值的医疗场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将覆盖分类法与双学习架构结合，可设计一个自适应系统，根据专家能力动态调整AI建议的呈现方式，从而提升人机协作效率。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量的结果标签和纵向数据，在数据稀疏或标签噪声大的环境中可能失效；此外，能力模型的估计偏差可能导致不恰当的模型更新。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从RLHF中获取灵感，但利用领域专家的覆盖行为作为更丰富的偏好信号，并引入能力分解来建模人类决策的局限性。

en.tldr: Reframes clinician overrides of AI recommendations as implicit preference data and proposes an extended preference learning framework with an override taxonomy, conditional preference formulation, and dual learning architecture to address suppression bias.
en.motivation: Clinician overrides contain rich domain knowledge and outcome information, but existing methods do not fully exploit this data to improve AI models.
en.method: Proposes a five-category override taxonomy, a preference formulation conditioned on patient state, organizational context, and clinician capability, and a dual learning architecture that jointly trains a reward model and a capability model.
en.result: The framework prevents suppression bias, where correct but difficult recommendations are systematically suppressed when clinician capability falls below an execution threshold.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers and practitioners leverage clinical override data to train AI models aligned with long-term patient trajectories, especially in value-based care settings.

idea_spark.idea: Combining the override taxonomy with the dual learning architecture could enable an adaptive system that dynamically adjusts AI recommendation presentation based on expert capability, improving human-AI collaboration.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality outcome labels and longitudinal data; may fail in data-sparse or noisy-label environments; biased capability estimates could lead to inappropriate model updates.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws inspiration from RLHF but uses domain expert overrides as richer preference signals, and introduces capability decomposition to model human decision-making limitations.

papers.title: Orbital Data Centers: Spacecraft Constraints and Economic Viability
papers.summary: Orbital data centers are being evaluated as solar-powered compute constellations and relay-integrated processing platforms. Their feasibility is not set by orbital solar flux alone, but by simultaneous closure of photovoltaic generation, eclipse recharge, radiative heat rejection, sustained space-to-ground communications, utilization, replacement cadence, and delivered compute-years over finite mission life. This paper derives necessary cluster-level competitiveness conditions using delivered information-technology (IT) electrical power $P_{\rm IT}$, deployed mass per delivered IT power $m_{\rm kW}$ in kg/kW, communication intensity $Γ=D_{\rm sg}/E_{\rm IT}$, sustained communication ceiling $Γ_{\max}$, effective utilization $U_{\rm eff}$, and lifetime penalty $Π_{\rm life}$. For a representative $P_{\rm IT}$=1 MW high-sunlight anchor, the base case gives beginning-of-life photovoltaic area $A^{\rm BOL}_{\rm PV}=5.64 \times 10^3 {\rm m}^2$, radiator area $A_{\rm rad}=2.50 \times 10^3 {\rm m^2}$, and 29.4 kg/kW for photovoltaic, storage, and radiator mass; fixed spacecraft mass raises the total to 34-59 kg/kW. At m_kW ~ 40 kg/kW, a terrestrial infrastructure benchmark of 10-40 k\$/kW allows only 250-1000 \$/kg for the combined launch and spacecraft-build cost before space-to-ground communications, operations, utilization, and lifetime terms are included. That allowance is 3.4-13.5 times below the current public Falcon 9 dedicated low-Earth-orbit launch-price benchmark alone, before spacecraft build is included. Space-native preprocessing and communications-integrated edge compute are credible early regimes; terrestrial-user general compute closes only for low Earth-coupled communication intensity, high effective utilization, long delivered lifetime, and very low combined launch-plus-build cost.

ai.tldr: 本文评估了轨道数据中心的可行性，指出其受光伏发电、日食充电、散热、通信、利用率和寿命等多重约束，并推导了集群级竞争力条件。
ai.motivation: 探索在轨道上部署太阳能计算星座和中继集成处理平台的可行性，并量化其经济竞争力。
ai.method: 推导了集群级竞争力条件，包括IT电力、单位质量功率、通信强度、有效利用率和寿命惩罚等指标，并以1 MW高日照锚点为例进行参数分析。
ai.result: 基准案例中，光伏面积5.64×10³ m²，散热器面积2.50×10³ m²，质量功率比29.4 kg/kW；总质量功率比34-59 kg/kW。在40 kg/kW时，允许的发射加建造总成本仅为250-1000 $/kg，远低于当前Falcon 9发射价格。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解轨道数据中心的物理约束和经济瓶颈，为相关技术路线选择提供参考。

idea_spark.idea: 提出一种结合空间预处理和通信集成边缘计算的早期应用模式，以降低对地面通用计算的依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 当前发射成本过高，且轨道数据中心的有效利用率和寿命难以达到经济闭合条件。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴地面数据中心的高效冷却和模块化设计，探索轨道环境下的新型散热和能源管理方案。

en.tldr: This paper evaluates orbital data centers, finding feasibility constrained by photovoltaic generation, eclipse recharge, heat rejection, communications, utilization, and lifetime, and derives cluster-level competitiveness conditions.
en.motivation: To explore the feasibility of deploying solar-powered compute constellations and relay-integrated processing platforms in orbit, and to quantify their economic competitiveness.
en.method: Derives cluster-level competitiveness conditions using metrics such as IT power, mass per power, communication intensity, effective utilization, and lifetime penalty, with a parametric analysis for a 1 MW high-sunlight anchor.
en.result: The base case yields photovoltaic area 5.64×10³ m², radiator area 2.50×10³ m², and mass per power 29.4 kg/kW; total mass per power 34-59 kg/kW. At 40 kg/kW, the allowed combined launch and build cost is only 250-1000 $/kg, far below current Falcon 9 launch prices.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the physical constraints and economic bottlenecks of orbital data centers, providing reference for technology roadmap selection.

idea_spark.idea: Propose an early application model combining space-native preprocessing and communications-integrated edge computing to reduce reliance on terrestrial general-purpose computing.
idea_spark.risk: Current launch costs are too high, and the effective utilization and lifetime of orbital data centers are unlikely to meet economic closure conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw on efficient cooling and modular design of terrestrial data centers to explore novel thermal management and energy solutions in orbital environments.

papers.title: treVM: Tiny Rust Embedded Virtual Machines with WASM on Variable Resource-Constrained Hardware
papers.summary: Software stacks embedded on microcontroller-based hardware typically provide rudimentary APIs programmed in C/C++, basic connectivity and, sometimes, a firmware update mechanism. Such coarse mechanisms contrast with widely used APIs and more advanced networked interaction expected from software stacks deployed on less resource-constrained hardware (microprocessor-based). In this paper, we aim to bridge this gap by designing treVM, a generic scheme to host high-level WebAssembly code capsules, bolted on a general-purpose Rust embedded software platform, able to run on a large variety of 32-bit microcontrollers. Not only can treVM capsules host highly customizable business logic, but capsules can also be securely updated on demand over the network, on devices already deployed in the field. We implement treVM in Rust, on top of Ariel OS, a general-purpose RTOS, and we publish the code as open source. Based on our implementation, we validate the feasibility of treVM on commonly available boards, and we report on extensive benchmarks we performed on heterogeneous hardware including Arm Cortex-M, RISC-V, and Xtensa microcontroller architectures. As such, treVM provides a promising new framework to secure continuous deployment of embedded software on low-power networked devices.

ai.tldr: 提出 treVM，一种在资源受限的 32 位微控制器上运行 WebAssembly 代码胶囊的通用方案，基于 Rust 和 Ariel OS，支持安全远程更新。
ai.motivation: 现有微控制器软件栈仅提供简陋的 C/C++ API 和有限的网络交互，无法满足高级业务逻辑和持续部署需求。
ai.method: 在 Rust 嵌入式平台（Ariel OS）上设计 treVM，将 WebAssembly 字节码作为可安全更新的代码胶囊，支持 Arm Cortex-M、RISC-V、Xtensa 等多种架构。
ai.result: 在多种常见开发板上验证可行性，并给出跨异构硬件的全面基准测试结果。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在低功耗联网设备上实现安全、可定制的持续部署。

idea_spark.idea: 利用 Rust 的内存安全特性和 WASM 的隔离性，构建一个可热更新的微控制器应用框架。
idea_spark.risk: WASM 解释器在极低资源 MCU 上的性能开销可能限制实时性要求高的场景。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受现代云原生“代码即数据”理念启发，将类似容器化部署模式引入嵌入式领域。

zh.tldr: 提出 treVM，一种在资源受限的 32 位微控制器上运行 WebAssembly 代码胶囊的通用方案，基于 Rust 和 Ariel OS，支持安全远程更新。
zh.motivation: 现有微控制器软件栈仅提供简陋的 C/C++ API 和有限的网络交互，无法满足高级业务逻辑和持续部署需求。
zh.method: 在 Rust 嵌入式平台（Ariel OS）上设计 treVM，将 WebAssembly 字节码作为可安全更新的代码胶囊，支持 Arm Cortex-M、RISC-V、Xtensa 等多种架构。
zh.result: 在多种常见开发板上验证可行性，并给出跨异构硬件的全面基准测试结果。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在低功耗联网设备上实现安全、可定制的持续部署。

idea_spark.idea: 利用 Rust 的内存安全特性和 WASM 的隔离性，构建一个可热更新的微控制器应用框架。
idea_spark.risk: WASM 解释器在极低资源 MCU 上的性能开销可能限制实时性要求高的场景。

en.tldr: treVM is a generic scheme to host WebAssembly code capsules on 32-bit microcontrollers using Rust and Ariel OS, enabling secure over-the-air updates.
en.motivation: Existing MCU software stacks offer only rudimentary C/C++ APIs and limited networking, hindering advanced business logic and continuous deployment.
en.method: Design treVM on top of Ariel OS (Rust-based RTOS), running WebAssembly bytecode as securely updatable capsules across Arm Cortex-M, RISC-V, and Xtensa architectures.
en.result: Feasibility validated on common boards with extensive benchmarks on heterogeneous hardware.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to achieve secure, customizable continuous deployment on low-power networked devices.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage Rust's memory safety and WASM isolation to build a hot-updatable application framework for microcontrollers.
idea_spark.risk: WASM interpreter overhead on extremely resource-constrained MCUs may limit real-time performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by modern cloud-native 'code-as-data' philosophy, bringing container-like deployment patterns to the embedded domain.

papers.title: End-to-End and Phase-Level Performance Optimization for Hyperledger Fabric
papers.summary: Hyperledger Fabric (HLF) is a modular, permissioned blockchain widely adopted in enterprise settings. Enhancing its throughput and latency remains challenging, as optimization decisions made in one phase of the transaction lifecycle can adversely affect other phases. In this work, we present a systematic, phase-level and end-to-end study of HLF optimizations along three fronts, combining production-grade testbed experiments with calibrated SimPy simulations. First, we introduce two novel optimization techniques that target commit-phase bottlenecks: block-level pipelining and strategic waiting. In pipelining, we overlap validation and private-data acquisition of successive blocks with state-consistency checks and ledger updates improving commit throughput by up to 1.9x. Strategic waiting coordinates commit progress by temporarily pausing fast leaders and boosting laggers to sustain endorsement parallelism, yielding up to a 1.2x higher throughput. Second, we conduct micro-benchmarking of three configuration levers: private-data dissemination, block-size selection, and endorsement peer selection. Our results reveal that: (i) Relaxed quorums for private-data dissemination significantly reduce latency in both endorsement and commit phases; (ii) Under light workloads, smaller blocks yield lower end-to-end latency, whereas, under heavy workloads, larger blocks are necessary to improve throughput and reduce latency; and (iii) Relaxed leader selection dramatically reduces dropped transactions and boosts endorsement throughput, with a modest increase in MVCC invalidations. Finally, we analyze the interplay among private-data dissemination, VSCC parallelization, and pipelined commits. Interestingly, the throughput gains over a serial commit path are maximized at a moderate level of parallelization. Together, our findings provide phase-aware and protocol-level refinements for optimizing HLF.

ai.tldr: 针对Hyperledger Fabric，提出分阶段与端到端的性能优化方法，包括区块级流水线、策略等待、以及配置杠杆的微基准测试。
ai.motivation: Hyperledger Fabric的优化决策在交易生命周期的不同阶段可能相互冲突，缺乏系统性研究。
ai.method: 结合生产级测试床实验与校准的SimPy仿真，从三个方向进行系统优化：引入区块级流水线和策略等待技术，微基准测试私有数据分发、区块大小和背书节点选择，分析并行化与流水线提交的交互。
ai.result: 区块级流水线提升提交吞吐量达1.9倍；策略等待提升吞吐量1.2倍；宽松的私有数据仲裁减少延迟；轻负载下小区块延迟更低，重负载下需大区块；宽松的领导者选择减少丢弃交易。
ai.help_to_user: 为Hyperledger Fabric的部署和调优提供分阶段、协议级的优化指导。

idea_spark.idea: 结合动态调整的区块大小和自适应领导者选择，实现运行时性能优化。
idea_spark.risk: 优化技术可能增加系统复杂性，需要仔细权衡并行化与一致性开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从操作系统流水线调度和分布式共识协议中汲取灵感，应用于区块链性能优化。

idea_spark.idea: 结合动态调整的区块大小和自适应领导者选择，实现运行时性能优化。
idea_spark.risk: 优化技术可能增加系统复杂性，需要仔细权衡并行化与一致性开销。

en.tldr: A systematic phase-level and end-to-end performance optimization study for Hyperledger Fabric, introducing block-level pipelining and strategic waiting, along with micro-benchmarking of configuration levers.
en.motivation: Optimization decisions in Hyperledger Fabric can adversely affect different phases of the transaction lifecycle, lacking a systematic study.
en.method: Combining production-grade testbed experiments with calibrated SimPy simulations, we optimize along three fronts: novel commit-phase techniques (block-level pipelining and strategic waiting), micro-benchmarking of private-data dissemination, block-size, and endorsement peer selection, and analysis of interplay among parallelization and pipelining.
en.result: Block-level pipelining improves commit throughput by up to 1.9x; strategic waiting yields up to 1.2x higher throughput; relaxed private-data quorums reduce latency; smaller blocks benefit light workloads while larger blocks are needed for heavy workloads; relaxed leader selection reduces dropped transactions.
en.help_to_user: Provides phase-aware and protocol-level refinements for optimizing Hyperledger Fabric deployments.

idea_spark.idea: Dynamic block-size adjustment and adaptive leader selection for runtime performance optimization.
idea_spark.risk: Increased system complexity and potential trade-offs between parallelism and consistency overhead.
idea_spark.inspiration: Drawing inspiration from OS pipeline scheduling and distributed consensus protocols for blockchain performance.
